Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive hChat Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. **Full File Tree of Source Directories**

```
/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/
├── main.rs                      (25 lines) - Entry point: eframe window init, ChatApp launch
├── app.rs                    (3,289 lines) - Core UI & state management; egui immediate-mode rendering
├── api.rs                      (682 lines) - OpenAI-compatible API client; SSE streaming, model fetching
├── message.rs                  (336 lines) - Message/Role types, content parts (Text/ImageUrl), ToolCall
├── storage.rs                (1,581 lines) - SQLite persistence; conversation/message/preset/draft CRUD
├── config.rs                   (242 lines) - TOML config loading/saving; sanitization & defaults
├── commands.rs                 (161 lines) - Slash command parser; /model, /temp, /system, /clear, /copy, /help
├── markdown.rs                 (335 lines) - Markdown segmentation; code blocks, reasoning blocks, prose
├── tools.rs                    (804 lines) - Tool def loader, TOML parser, 5 builtins + shell handler
└── default_tools/              (5 TOML files)
    ├── read_file.toml          - Builtin: file reads with offset/limit
    ├── list_directory.toml     - Builtin: directory listing
    ├── search_files.toml       - Builtin: regex search (text files only)
    ├── write_file.toml         - Builtin: file writes (confirm safety)
    └── run_shell.toml          - Builtin: shell execution (confirm safety, 5min timeout)

Total Rust LOC: 7,455 lines | Codebase complexity: Moderate (well-modularized, egui + async)
```

---

### 2. **Cargo.toml — Dependencies & Build Profile**

**Binary name:** `hchat` | **Edition:** 2024 | **Rust version:** 1.85+  
**Version:** 0.9.4

**Key Dependencies:**
- `eframe` 0.34 — egui application framework
- `egui_commonmark` 0.23 — markdown rendering
- `reqwest` 0.12 — HTTP client with streaming
- `tokio` 1.x — async runtime (multi-threaded, macros, sync channels)
- `tokio-util` 0.7 — CancellationToken for stream cancellation
- `futures-util` 0.3 — StreamExt for byte streaming
- `rusqlite` 0.31 (bundled SQLite) — conversation/message persistence
- `serde`/`serde_json` 1.x — JSON serialization
- `toml` 0.8 — config file parsing
- `tiktoken-rs` 0.11 — token counting (live counter, cost estimation)
- `base64` 0.22 — image data URL encoding
- `arboard` 3 — clipboard access
- `regex` 1 — file search pattern matching
- `font-kit` 0.14.3 — system font lookup (Linux fontconfig, macOS Core Text)
- `dirs` 6 — platform config/data dirs

**Build Profiles:**
- `release`: `opt-level="s"` (size), LTO, symbol stripping → minimal binary
- `dist`: inherits from release with aggressive optimization

---

### 3. **Key Structs & Functions — Core Architecture**

#### **main.rs**
```rust
fn main() -> eframe::Result<()>
// - Loads Config from ~/.config/hchat/config.toml
// - Sets up 900×600 viewport titled "hChat"
// - Spawns ChatApp on the tokio runtime
```

#### **app.rs — ChatApp struct (144 fields)**

**UI/Display State:**
- `messages: Vec<Message>` — in-memory conversation active path
- `input: String` — user text input buffer
- `show_sidebar, show_context_sidebar, show_find` — panel visibility toggles
- `commonmark_cache` — egui markdown renderer state

**Model & Generation:**
- `base_url: String` — current endpoint URL
- `models: Vec<String>` — available models at endpoint
- `selected_model: usize` — index into models
- `system_prompt: String`, `temperature: f32`, `max_tokens: u32` — per-conv settings
- `top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty: Option<f32>`, `stop_sequences: Vec<String>` — advanced sampling
- `use_max_tokens: bool` — flag to send max_tokens to API

**Streaming & Async:**
- `runtime: tokio::runtime::Runtime` — embedded tokio for spawning API tasks
- `rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>` — chat stream events
- `models_rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>` — model fetch events
- `title_rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<(i64, Result<String, String>)>>` — auto-title channel
- `cancel_token: Option<CancellationToken>` — stream cancellation
- `streaming: bool` — active streaming flag
- `error: Option<String>` — error toast

**Persistence & History:**
- `storage: Storage` — SQLite handle
- `current_conversation_id: Option<i64>` — active conv DB row
- `conversation_list: Vec<(i64, String, bool)>` — sidebar (id, title, pinned)
- `settings_signature: u64` — dirty-check hash for per-conv settings flush
- `last_seen_input, persisted_draft, draft_dirty_since` — debounced draft persist (500ms)

**Settings & Config:**
- `saved_endpoints: Vec<Endpoint>` — URL + api_key pairs
- `show_endpoints: bool`, `new_endpoint: String`, `new_endpoint_key: String` — endpoint manager
- `config: Config` — global defaults (system_prompt, temperature, fonts, theme)
- `dark_mode: bool`, `presets_cache: Vec<Preset>`, `show_presets: bool` — UI customization
- `token_cache: Option<(u64, usize)>` — tiktoken signature + count (recompute only on change)

**Tool Execution (Phase 5a):**
- `loaded_tools: Vec<ToolDef>` — tools from ~/.config/hchat/tools/ loaded on startup
- `pending_tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>` — queue of calls to execute
- `pending_approval: Option<ToolCall>` — parked call awaiting user confirmation
- `auto_approved_tools: HashSet<String>` — per-session allowlist (not persisted)
- `tool_iterations: u32` — counter for tool-then-restream cycles (capped at 8)

**Search & Navigation:**
- `search_query: String`, `search_results: Vec<(i64, String, String)>`, `show_search: bool`
- `find_query: String`, `last_scrolled_find_query: Option<String>` — find-in-conversation
- `renaming_conversation: Option<i64>, rename_buffer: String` — inline rename

**Message Editing & Branching:**
- `editing_message: Option<usize>, edit_buffer: String` — edit mode for messages
- `pending_attachments: Vec<(String, ContentPart)>` — image/text files staged for next send
- `sibling_info_cache: Vec<Option<(usize, usize)>>` — per-frame cache of (current_pos, total_siblings)
- `working_dir: String` — per-conversation base path for tool calls

**Cost Tracking:**
- `last_usage: Option<Usage>` — prompt/completion tokens + cost from last response
- `session_cost: f64` — cumulative cost since app launch

---

#### **Key ChatApp Methods:**

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `new()` | Construct app, load font/theme, init DB, fetch models |
| `send_message()` | Build user message (text + attachments), start streaming |
| `start_streaming()` | Spawn API task with tokio, push empty assistant placeholder |
| `process_events()` | Drain rx/models_rx/title_rx channels, apply streamed content to messages |
| `fetch_models()` | Hit `/v1/models` (OpenAI) or `/api/tags` (Ollama) |
| `new_conversation()` | Clear state, reset settings to defaults |
| `load_conversation(id)` | Load from DB, restore per-conv settings |
| `save_current()` | Incremental INSERT/UPDATE to messages, CREATE conv on first save |
| `regenerate()` | Pop assistant, push new placeholder, restart streaming (branches) |
| `edit_and_resend(index)` | Truncate to message N, push edited user message, stream (branches) |
| `navigate_sibling(index, direction)` | Walk sibling branches via DB queries, splice into memory |
| `persist_draft_if_idle()` | Debounce 500ms, write input buffer to `conversations.draft` |
| `reload_config()` | Reload TOML, hot-swap fonts/theme, re-fetch models if endpoint changed |
| `kick_off_tool_execution()` | Extract tool_calls from last assistant message, start approval/execution loop |
| `process_pending_tool_calls()` | Drain auto-safety tools, park confirm tools in `pending_approval` |
| `execute_tool_call()` | Dispatch to builtin Rust or shell subprocess, capture result |

---

#### **api.rs**

**Structs:**
```rust
pub struct ChatParams {
    base_url, model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, top_p, ..., tools
}
pub enum StreamEvent {
    Token(String),              // Content delta
    Reasoning(String),          // Separate reasoning field (DeepSeek, Qwen3, OpenRouter)
    ToolCalls(Vec<ToolCall>),   // Assembled tool calls
    UsageInfo(Usage),           // Prompt/completion tokens + cost
    ModelsLoaded { url, models }, // Model list from endpoint
    Done,
    Error(String),
}
```

**Core Functions:**
```rust
pub async fn fetch_models(base_url: &str, api_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>
// - Tries OpenAI /v1/models first
// - Falls back to Ollama /api/tags
// - Auto-fixes OpenRouter URLs (substring-attack prevention)
// - Returns helpful error if no models found ("pull a model first")

pub fn stream_chat(params: ChatParams, tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<StreamEvent>, cancel: CancellationToken)
// - Spawns tokio task to handle streaming
// - Opens HTTP stream to /chat/completions
// - Parses SSE "data: {...}" line-by-line with UTF-8 boundary handling
// - Accumulates tool_calls piecewise across chunks (indexed by slot)
// - Emits StreamEvent per delta
// - Handles provider-specific fields: reasoning, tool_calls, usage

pub async fn complete_once(base_url: &str, model: &str, messages: &[Message], ...) -> Result<String, String>
// - Non-streaming completion (used by auto-title)
// - Thin wrapper around /chat/completions with stream=false
```

**SSE Parsing Logic (lines 374–478):**
- Buffers incoming bytes with UTF-8 validity checks
- Finds newlines, extracts "data: " lines
- Parses JSON ChatChunk, extracts content/reasoning/tool_calls deltas
- Tool call accumulator: `Vec<Option<(id, name, args_so_far)>>` keyed by slot index
- Flushes assembled ToolCalls before Done
- Handles provider variants: empty content strings, out-of-order tool deltas, reasoning field

**Error Handling:**
- User-friendly HTTP status messages (401 auth, 404 endpoint, 429 rate limit, 5xx)
- Max stream buffer (1MB); max error body logged (4KB)
- Timeouts: 30s request, 10s connect

---

#### **message.rs**

**Core Types:**
```rust
pub enum Role { System, User, Assistant, Tool }

pub enum ContentPart {
    Text { text: String },
    ImageUrl { image_url: ImageUrl }  // data: or https:// URLs
}

pub struct Message {
    pub role: Role,
    pub content: Vec<ContentPart>,
    pub tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>>,      // Only on assistant
    pub tool_call_id: Option<String>,           // Only on tool messages
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,                // Unix ms, session-local
    pub id: Option<i64>,                        // SQLite rowid, None = unsaved
    pub parent_id: Option<i64>,                 // Branching tree linkage
    pub branch_index: i64,                      // Sibling counter
}

pub struct ToolCall {
    pub id: String,
    pub call_type: String,          // Always "function"
    pub function: ToolCallFunction {
        pub name: String,
        pub arguments: String,      // JSON-encoded, unparsed
    }
}
```

**Methods:**
- `Message::text(role, body)` — quick constructor for text-only messages
- `Message::from_parts(role, parts)` — multimodal constructor
- `Message::tool_result(call_id, body)` — role=Tool message
- `Message::text_str()` — concatenate all Text parts, skip ImageUrl
- `Message::append_text(s)` — extend trailing Text part or push new one (used during streaming)
- `Message::is_empty_content()` — true if all parts are empty/missing
- `Message::images()` — iterator over ImageUrl parts

All types serialize directly to OpenAI chat API format (serde derive).

---

#### **storage.rs — SQLite Schema (v2)**

**Schema (v2 migration adds columns on launch):**

```sql
-- Baseline (v0.6.0 shape)
conversations: id, title, created_at, updated_at
messages: id, conversation_id, role, content, position
presets: id, name, created_at

-- Additions (v1, then v2)
conversations: +model, +system_prompt, +temperature, +max_tokens, +use_max_tokens,
               +top_p, +frequency_penalty, +presence_penalty, +stop_sequences,
               +endpoint, +pinned, +draft, +auto_titled, +working_dir
messages: +parent_id, +branch_index, +created_at, +tool_calls, +tool_call_id
presets: +model, +system_prompt, +temperature, +max_tokens, +use_max_tokens,
         +top_p, +frequency_penalty, +presence_penalty, +stop_sequences, +endpoint
```

**Indexes:**
```sql
idx_messages_conv_position (conversation_id, position)
idx_messages_parent (parent_id)
idx_conversations_pinned_updated (pinned DESC, updated_at DESC)
```

**Key Structs:**
```rust
pub struct ConversationSettings {
    model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens,
    top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences,
    endpoint, working_dir  // All Option<T> for backward compat
}

pub struct Storage {
    conn: Connection  // PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; busy_timeout(5s)
}
```

**Key Methods:**
| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `save_messages(conv_id, messages)` | Incremental INSERT (id=None) or UPDATE (id=Some). Auto-links parent_id to prior message. |
| `load_messages(conv_id)` | Walks branching tree: newest root, then newest sibling at each step. Falls back to legacy position order if no parent_ids set. |
| `load_conversation_settings(conv_id)` | Loads all per-conv columns; None fields use global defaults. |
| `save_conversation_settings(conv_id, settings)` | Persists snapshot to conv row. |
| `list_conversations()` | Returns (id, title, pinned) tuples, ordered by pinned DESC, updated_at DESC. |
| `next_branch_index(conv_id, parent_id)` | MAX(branch_index) + 1 for new siblings. |
| `siblings_of(message_id)` | Returns all messages with the same parent_id + created_at. |
| `walk_from(message_id)` | Walks forward from a leaf through its ancestors to construct a full path. |
| `backfill_parent_ids(conv_id)` | One-shot migration for legacy 0.7.0 conversations. |

**Draft Persistence:**
```rust
pub fn save_draft(conv_id, text)   // UPDATE conversations SET draft = text
pub fn load_draft(conv_id) -> Option<String>  // SELECT draft FROM conversations WHERE id
```

**Preset CRUD:**
```rust
pub fn create_preset(name, settings) -> Result<i64>
pub fn list_presets() -> Vec<Preset>
pub fn delete_preset(id)
pub fn apply_preset_to_conversation(conv_id, preset_id)
```

**Auto-Title Flow:**
```rust
pub fn needs_auto_title(conv_id) -> bool     // CHECK auto_titled IS 0
pub fn mark_auto_titled(conv_id)             // SET auto_titled = 1
pub fn update_conversation_title(conv_id, new_title)
```

---

#### **config.rs**

**Config Struct:**
```rust
pub struct Config {
    // Appearance
    font_size: f32,           // 8.0..=24.0, default 14
    mono_font_size: f32,      // 8.0..=24.0, default 13
    ui_scale: f32,            // 0.75..=2.0, default 1.0
    dark_mode: bool,          // default true
    font_family: String,      // System font lookup, empty = builtin
    mono_font_family: String,
    
    // Generation
    system_prompt: String,    // Prepended to all new convs
    temperature: f32,         // 0.0..=2.0, default 0.7
    max_tokens: u32,          // 64..=16384, default 2048
    use_max_tokens: bool,     // Whether to send it to API
    
    // Sampling (all optional, None = use model default)
    top_p: Option<f32>,                    // 0.0..=1.0
    frequency_penalty: Option<f32>,        // -2.0..=2.0
    presence_penalty: Option<f32>,         // -2.0..=2.0
    stop_sequences: Vec<String>,           // Max 4, max 256 chars each
    
    // Endpoints
    default_endpoint: String,              // Must match a saved_endpoints URL
    saved_endpoints: Vec<Endpoint>
}

pub struct Endpoint {
    pub url: String,           // Base URL (http://localhost:1234/v1)
    pub api_key: Option<String> // Bearer auth if present
}
```

**Load/Save:**
- **Path:** `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` (Linux) / `~/Library/Preferences/hchat/config.toml` (macOS, via `dirs`)
- **Behavior:** On first launch, seed with `example.config.toml` bundled in binary; on corrupt parse, back up to `.toml.broken-<timestamp>` and use defaults
- **Sanitization:** Clamps float ranges, validates endpoint list, truncates stop sequences
- **Hot reload:** Called on `reload_config()` to pick up TOML edits without restart

---

#### **commands.rs**

**Slash Command Parser:**
```rust
pub enum Command {
    Model(String),       // /model <substring>
    Temperature(f32),    // /temp 0.7
    System(String),      // /system <text>
    Clear,               // /clear
    Copy,                // /copy
    Help,                // /help
}

pub fn parse(input: &str) -> ParseResult
// - Checks if starts with '/'
// - Substring-matches model names
// - Validates temp as finite float in [0, 2]
// - Treats unknown verbs as errors (not silent sends)
```

**Aliases:**
- `/model`, `/m`
- `/temp`, `/temperature`, `/t`
- `/system`, `/sys`
- `/clear`, `/new`
- `/copy`
- `/help`, `/?`, `/h`

---

#### **markdown.rs**

**Segment-based Rendering:**
```rust
pub enum Segment<'a> {
    Markdown(&'a str),                  // Prose for egui_commonmark
    Code { lang: &'a str, body: &'a str }, // Fenced ```lang ... ```
    Reasoning { body: &'a str, closed: bool }, // <think>...</think>
}

pub fn segments(text: &str) -> Vec<Segment<'_>>
// Walks text, splits on code fences and <think> blocks
// Returns segments covering the whole input
// Markdown semantics do NOT survive splits (lists restart, etc.)
```

**Helper Functions:**
- `strip_reasoning(text) -> String` — removes <think>...</think> blocks
- Used for auto-title generation (model thinks but we only want the title text)

---

#### **tools.rs — Tool Definition & Execution**

**ToolDef & Safety:**
```rust
pub struct ToolDef {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,  // JSON Schema, passed through to API
    pub handler: Handler,
    pub safety: Safety,  // Auto | Confirm
}

pub enum Handler {
    Builtin(BuiltinRef),              // "builtin:read_file"
    Shell { shell: Vec<String> },     // ["git", "log", "-n", "{{count}}"]
}

pub enum Safety {
    Auto,    // Execute silently (read-only tools)
    Confirm, // Pop approval card (write_file, run_shell)
}
```

**5 Default Tools (seeded on first launch to `~/.config/hchat/tools/`):**

| Tool | Safety | Handler | Limits |
|------|--------|---------|--------|
| `read_file` | auto | builtin | 100 KB output |
| `list_directory` | auto | builtin | — |
| `search_files` | auto | builtin | 200 matches, skips .dotdirs + binary |
| `write_file` | confirm | builtin | Creates parent dirs |
| `run_shell` | confirm | builtin | 300s wall-clock timeout, 50 KB output |

**Execution:**
```rust
pub fn execute_tool_call(call: &ToolCall) -> String
// - Looks up ToolDef by name
// - Parses call.arguments as JSON
// - Dispatches to Handler::Builtin or Handler::Shell
// - Returns result string or error message

pub fn run_builtin(name, args, working_dir) -> Result<String>
// builtin_read_file, builtin_list_directory, builtin_search_files,
// builtin_write_file, builtin_run_shell

pub fn run_shell_tool(template: &[String], args: &Value, wd: &Path) -> Result<String>
// Substitutes {{varname}} placeholders from args
// Execs with working_dir as cwd
// Captures stdout + stderr + exit code
```

**Custom Tools:**
Users drop `.toml` files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. Format:
```toml
name = "my_tool"
description = "Does something"
safety = "confirm"  # or "auto"
parameters = { ... JSON Schema ... }

# Either:
handler = "builtin:read_file"
# Or:
handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
```

Tools reload on app restart (Phase 5a; `/tools-reload` command not yet implemented).

### 5. **Overall Line Count & Complexity**

**Codebase Metrics:**
- **Total Rust:** 7,455 lines across 9 modules
- **Largest:** app.rs (3,289 lines) — the immediate-mode UI loop
- **Distribution:** Storage (1,581) < App (3,289) < API (682) < Tools (804) < others
- **Complexity:** Moderate
  - Async/await with tokio runtime embedded in the app struct
  - Branching conversation tree stored in SQLite with in-memory navigation
  - SSE streaming with incremental token/tool-call parsing
  - egui immediate-mode UI with 150+ fields in ChatApp
  - Schema migration on launch (v0→v2 ALTERs)

**No external Rust bindings** to C/C++ (SQLite bundled via rusqlite, font lookup via font-kit).

---

### 6. **Config Files & Assets**

**Config Layer 1 — Bundled Defaults:**
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/example.config.toml` — template, seeded on first launch to `~/.config/hchat/config.toml`

**Config Layer 2 — Per-Conversation:**
- SQLite `conversations` table columns: model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences, endpoint, working_dir

**Assets:**
- Default tool TOMLs bundled in binary at compile time (lines in `tools.rs`): read_file, list_directory, search_files, write_file, run_shell
- System fonts loaded at runtime via font-kit

**Platform-Specific Code:**
- `dirs::config_dir()` / `dirs::data_dir()` — cross-platform config/data paths (Linux ~/.config, ~/.local/share; macOS ~/Library/Preferences, ~/Library/Application Support)
- Unix-only: `fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o700)` in config.rs and storage.rs (directories created with 700 perms)
- eframe/egui handle Vulkan/OpenGL on Linux, Metal on macOS automatically

---

### 7. **Key Design Patterns**

**Async Bridging:**
- Single `tokio::runtime::Runtime` embedded in ChatApp lives for the app's lifetime
- API calls spawned as tasks (`runtime.spawn(async { ... })`)
- Results flow back via `mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>` (no sync boundary — channels live inside ChatApp)
- `process_events()` drains channels every frame; items applied to in-memory messages
- CancellationToken for stop/cancel during streaming

**Incremental Save:**
- Messages marked `id=None` are INSERT'd (rowid written back into the struct)
- Messages marked `id=Some` are UPDATE'd in place
- Parent linkage auto-linked to prior message in slice (no caller threading)
- Siblings preserved on disk; active path walked on load

**Branching Navigation:**
- Every message has `parent_id` (None = root), `branch_index` (sibling counter)
- Siblings indexed by (parent_id, branch_index, created_at DESC)
- Load path: walk from newest root, pick newest sibling at each step
- No explicit "branch" entity — just message tree with multi-parent support

**Streaming Resilience:**
- UTF-8 boundary aware byte buffering (don't split multibyte chars)
- Tool call accumulation by slot index across chunks
- Empty content/reasoning deltas skipped (provider keep-alives)
- Stream buffer cap (1MB) and error body truncation (4KB)

**Event Sourcing (Implicit):**
- Messages immutable once saved (UPDATE only on content for streaming appends)
- Presets are snapshots of ConversationSettings, cloned on apply
- Drafts persisted inline in conversations row (not a separate table)

---

### 8. **File Paths (Absolute)**

**Source:**
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/main.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/api.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/message.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/storage.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/config.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/commands.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/markdown.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/tools.rs`

**Config/Data (Runtime):**
- `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — global settings
- `~/.config/hchat/tools/*.toml` — user-defined tools
- `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux) / `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) — SQLite DB

**Build Artifact:**
- `target/release/hchat` — optimized binary

---

This codebase is a well-structured Rust desktop app with good separation of concerns: UI (app.rs), networking (api.rs), persistence (storage.rs), and tooling (tools.rs). The phase-based versioning and incremental feature additions show deliberate design. Ready for a major rewrite!

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive hChat Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. **Full File Tree of Source Directories**

```
/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/
├── main.rs                      (25 lines) - Entry point: eframe window init, ChatApp launch
├── app.rs                    (3,289 lines) - Core UI & state management; egui immediate-mode rendering
├── api.rs                      (682 lines) - OpenAI-compatible API client; SSE streaming, model fetching
├── message.rs                  (336 lines) - Message/Role types, content parts (Text/ImageUrl), ToolCall
├── storage.rs                (1,581 lines) - SQLite persistence; conversation/message/preset/draft CRUD
├── config.rs                   (242 lines) - TOML config loading/saving; sanitization & defaults
├── commands.rs                 (161 lines) - Slash command parser; /model, /temp, /system, /clear, /copy, /help
├── markdown.rs                 (335 lines) - Markdown segmentation; code blocks, reasoning blocks, prose
├── tools.rs                    (804 lines) - Tool def loader, TOML parser, 5 builtins + shell handler
└── default_tools/              (5 TOML files)
    ├── read_file.toml          - Builtin: file reads with offset/limit
    ├── list_directory.toml     - Builtin: directory listing
    ├── search_files.toml       - Builtin: regex search (text files only)
    ├── write_file.toml         - Builtin: file writes (confirm safety)
    └── run_shell.toml          - Builtin: shell execution (confirm safety, 5min timeout)

Total Rust LOC: 7,455 lines | Codebase complexity: Moderate (well-modularized, egui + async)
```

---

### 2. **Cargo.toml — Dependencies & Build Profile**

**Binary name:** `hchat` | **Edition:** 2024 | **Rust version:** 1.85+  
**Version:** 0.9.4

**Key Dependencies:**
- `eframe` 0.34 — egui application framework
- `egui_commonmark` 0.23 — markdown rendering
- `reqwest` 0.12 — HTTP client with streaming
- `tokio` 1.x — async runtime (multi-threaded, macros, sync channels)
- `tokio-util` 0.7 — CancellationToken for stream cancellation
- `futures-util` 0.3 — StreamExt for byte streaming
- `rusqlite` 0.31 (bundled SQLite) — conversation/message persistence
- `serde`/`serde_json` 1.x — JSON serialization
- `toml` 0.8 — config file parsing
- `tiktoken-rs` 0.11 — token counting (live counter, cost estimation)
- `base64` 0.22 — image data URL encoding
- `arboard` 3 — clipboard access
- `regex` 1 — file search pattern matching
- `font-kit` 0.14.3 — system font lookup (Linux fontconfig, macOS Core Text)
- `dirs` 6 — platform config/data dirs

**Build Profiles:**
- `release`: `opt-level="s"` (size), LTO, symbol stripping → minimal binary
- `dist`: inherits from release with aggressive optimization

---

### 3. **Key Structs & Functions — Core Architecture**

#### **main.rs**
```rust
fn main() -> eframe::Result<()>
// - Loads Config from ~/.config/hchat/config.toml
// - Sets up 900×600 viewport titled "hChat"
// - Spawns ChatApp on the tokio runtime
```

#### **app.rs — ChatApp struct (144 fields)**

**UI/Display State:**
- `messages: Vec<Message>` — in-memory conversation active path
- `input: String` — user text input buffer
- `show_sidebar, show_context_sidebar, show_find` — panel visibility toggles
- `commonmark_cache` — egui markdown renderer state

**Model & Generation:**
- `base_url: String` — current endpoint URL
- `models: Vec<String>` — available models at endpoint
- `selected_model: usize` — index into models
- `system_prompt: String`, `temperature: f32`, `max_tokens: u32` — per-conv settings
- `top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty: Option<f32>`, `stop_sequences: Vec<String>` — advanced sampling
- `use_max_tokens: bool` — flag to send max_tokens to API

**Streaming & Async:**
- `runtime: tokio::runtime::Runtime` — embedded tokio for spawning API tasks
- `rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>` — chat stream events
- `models_rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>` — model fetch events
- `title_rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<(i64, Result<String, String>)>>` — auto-title channel
- `cancel_token: Option<CancellationToken>` — stream cancellation
- `streaming: bool` — active streaming flag
- `error: Option<String>` — error toast

**Persistence & History:**
- `storage: Storage` — SQLite handle
- `current_conversation_id: Option<i64>` — active conv DB row
- `conversation_list: Vec<(i64, String, bool)>` — sidebar (id, title, pinned)
- `settings_signature: u64` — dirty-check hash for per-conv settings flush
- `last_seen_input, persisted_draft, draft_dirty_since` — debounced draft persist (500ms)

**Settings & Config:**
- `saved_endpoints: Vec<Endpoint>` — URL + api_key pairs
- `show_endpoints: bool`, `new_endpoint: String`, `new_endpoint_key: String` — endpoint manager
- `config: Config` — global defaults (system_prompt, temperature, fonts, theme)
- `dark_mode: bool`, `presets_cache: Vec<Preset>`, `show_presets: bool` — UI customization
- `token_cache: Option<(u64, usize)>` — tiktoken signature + count (recompute only on change)

**Tool Execution (Phase 5a):**
- `loaded_tools: Vec<ToolDef>` — tools from ~/.config/hchat/tools/ loaded on startup
- `pending_tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>` — queue of calls to execute
- `pending_approval: Option<ToolCall>` — parked call awaiting user confirmation
- `auto_approved_tools: HashSet<String>` — per-session allowlist (not persisted)
- `tool_iterations: u32` — counter for tool-then-restream cycles (capped at 8)

**Search & Navigation:**
- `search_query: String`, `search_results: Vec<(i64, String, String)>`, `show_search: bool`
- `find_query: String`, `last_scrolled_find_query: Option<String>` — find-in-conversation
- `renaming_conversation: Option<i64>, rename_buffer: String` — inline rename

**Message Editing & Branching:**
- `editing_message: Option<usize>, edit_buffer: String` — edit mode for messages
- `pending_attachments: Vec<(String, ContentPart)>` — image/text files staged for next send
- `sibling_info_cache: Vec<Option<(usize, usize)>>` — per-frame cache of (current_pos, total_siblings)
- `working_dir: String` — per-conversation base path for tool calls

**Cost Tracking:**
- `last_usage: Option<Usage>` — prompt/completion tokens + cost from last response
- `session_cost: f64` — cumulative cost since app launch

---

#### **Key ChatApp Methods:**

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `new()` | Construct app, load font/theme, init DB, fetch models |
| `send_message()` | Build user message (text + attachments), start streaming |
| `start_streaming()` | Spawn API task with tokio, push empty assistant placeholder |
| `process_events()` | Drain rx/models_rx/title_rx channels, apply streamed content to messages |
| `fetch_models()` | Hit `/v1/models` (OpenAI) or `/api/tags` (Ollama) |
| `new_conversation()` | Clear state, reset settings to defaults |
| `load_conversation(id)` | Load from DB, restore per-conv settings |
| `save_current()` | Incremental INSERT/UPDATE to messages, CREATE conv on first save |
| `regenerate()` | Pop assistant, push new placeholder, restart streaming (branches) |
| `edit_and_resend(index)` | Truncate to message N, push edited user message, stream (branches) |
| `navigate_sibling(index, direction)` | Walk sibling branches via DB queries, splice into memory |
| `persist_draft_if_idle()` | Debounce 500ms, write input buffer to `conversations.draft` |
| `reload_config()` | Reload TOML, hot-swap fonts/theme, re-fetch models if endpoint changed |
| `kick_off_tool_execution()` | Extract tool_calls from last assistant message, start approval/execution loop |
| `process_pending_tool_calls()` | Drain auto-safety tools, park confirm tools in `pending_approval` |
| `execute_tool_call()` | Dispatch to builtin Rust or shell subprocess, capture result |

---

#### **api.rs**

**Structs:**
```rust
pub struct ChatParams {
    base_url, model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, top_p, ..., tools
}
pub enum StreamEvent {
    Token(String),              // Content delta
    Reasoning(String),          // Separate reasoning field (DeepSeek, Qwen3, OpenRouter)
    ToolCalls(Vec<ToolCall>),   // Assembled tool calls
    UsageInfo(Usage),           // Prompt/completion tokens + cost
    ModelsLoaded { url, models }, // Model list from endpoint
    Done,
    Error(String),
}
```

**Core Functions:**
```rust
pub async fn fetch_models(base_url: &str, api_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>
// - Tries OpenAI /v1/models first
// - Falls back to Ollama /api/tags
// - Auto-fixes OpenRouter URLs (substring-attack prevention)
// - Returns helpful error if no models found ("pull a model first")

pub fn stream_chat(params: ChatParams, tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<StreamEvent>, cancel: CancellationToken)
// - Spawns tokio task to handle streaming
// - Opens HTTP stream to /chat/completions
// - Parses SSE "data: {...}" line-by-line with UTF-8 boundary handling
// - Accumulates tool_calls piecewise across chunks (indexed by slot)
// - Emits StreamEvent per delta
// - Handles provider-specific fields: reasoning, tool_calls, usage

pub async fn complete_once(base_url: &str, model: &str, messages: &[Message], ...) -> Result<String, String>
// - Non-streaming completion (used by auto-title)
// - Thin wrapper around /chat/completions with stream=false
```

**SSE Parsing Logic (lines 374–478):**
- Buffers incoming bytes with UTF-8 validity checks
- Finds newlines, extracts "data: " lines
- Parses JSON ChatChunk, extracts content/reasoning/tool_calls deltas
- Tool call accumulator: `Vec<Option<(id, name, args_so_far)>>` keyed by slot index
- Flushes assembled ToolCalls before Done
- Handles provider variants: empty content strings, out-of-order tool deltas, reasoning field

**Error Handling:**
- User-friendly HTTP status messages (401 auth, 404 endpoint, 429 rate limit, 5xx)
- Max stream buffer (1MB); max error body logged (4KB)
- Timeouts: 30s request, 10s connect

---

#### **message.rs**

**Core Types:**
```rust
pub enum Role { System, User, Assistant, Tool }

pub enum ContentPart {
    Text { text: String },
    ImageUrl { image_url: ImageUrl }  // data: or https:// URLs
}

pub struct Message {
    pub role: Role,
    pub content: Vec<ContentPart>,
    pub tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>>,      // Only on assistant
    pub tool_call_id: Option<String>,           // Only on tool messages
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,                // Unix ms, session-local
    pub id: Option<i64>,                        // SQLite rowid, None = unsaved
    pub parent_id: Option<i64>,                 // Branching tree linkage
    pub branch_index: i64,                      // Sibling counter
}

pub struct ToolCall {
    pub id: String,
    pub call_type: String,          // Always "function"
    pub function: ToolCallFunction {
        pub name: String,
        pub arguments: String,      // JSON-encoded, unparsed
    }
}
```

**Methods:**
- `Message::text(role, body)` — quick constructor for text-only messages
- `Message::from_parts(role, parts)` — multimodal constructor
- `Message::tool_result(call_id, body)` — role=Tool message
- `Message::text_str()` — concatenate all Text parts, skip ImageUrl
- `Message::append_text(s)` — extend trailing Text part or push new one (used during streaming)
- `Message::is_empty_content()` — true if all parts are empty/missing
- `Message::images()` — iterator over ImageUrl parts

All types serialize directly to OpenAI chat API format (serde derive).

---

#### **storage.rs — SQLite Schema (v2)**

**Schema (v2 migration adds columns on launch):**

```sql
-- Baseline (v0.6.0 shape)
conversations: id, title, created_at, updated_at
messages: id, conversation_id, role, content, position
presets: id, name, created_at

-- Additions (v1, then v2)
conversations: +model, +system_prompt, +temperature, +max_tokens, +use_max_tokens,
               +top_p, +frequency_penalty, +presence_penalty, +stop_sequences,
               +endpoint, +pinned, +draft, +auto_titled, +working_dir
messages: +parent_id, +branch_index, +created_at, +tool_calls, +tool_call_id
presets: +model, +system_prompt, +temperature, +max_tokens, +use_max_tokens,
         +top_p, +frequency_penalty, +presence_penalty, +stop_sequences, +endpoint
```

**Indexes:**
```sql
idx_messages_conv_position (conversation_id, position)
idx_messages_parent (parent_id)
idx_conversations_pinned_updated (pinned DESC, updated_at DESC)
```

**Key Structs:**
```rust
pub struct ConversationSettings {
    model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens,
    top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences,
    endpoint, working_dir  // All Option<T> for backward compat
}

pub struct Storage {
    conn: Connection  // PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; busy_timeout(5s)
}
```

**Key Methods:**
| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `save_messages(conv_id, messages)` | Incremental INSERT (id=None) or UPDATE (id=Some). Auto-links parent_id to prior message. |
| `load_messages(conv_id)` | Walks branching tree: newest root, then newest sibling at each step. Falls back to legacy position order if no parent_ids set. |
| `load_conversation_settings(conv_id)` | Loads all per-conv columns; None fields use global defaults. |
| `save_conversation_settings(conv_id, settings)` | Persists snapshot to conv row. |
| `list_conversations()` | Returns (id, title, pinned) tuples, ordered by pinned DESC, updated_at DESC. |
| `next_branch_index(conv_id, parent_id)` | MAX(branch_index) + 1 for new siblings. |
| `siblings_of(message_id)` | Returns all messages with the same parent_id + created_at. |
| `walk_from(message_id)` | Walks forward from a leaf through its ancestors to construct a full path. |
| `backfill_parent_ids(conv_id)` | One-shot migration for legacy 0.7.0 conversations. |

**Draft Persistence:**
```rust
pub fn save_draft(conv_id, text)   // UPDATE conversations SET draft = text
pub fn load_draft(conv_id) -> Option<String>  // SELECT draft FROM conversations WHERE id
```

**Preset CRUD:**
```rust
pub fn create_preset(name, settings) -> Result<i64>
pub fn list_presets() -> Vec<Preset>
pub fn delete_preset(id)
pub fn apply_preset_to_conversation(conv_id, preset_id)
```

**Auto-Title Flow:**

---

### 5. **Overall Line Count & Complexity**

**Codebase Metrics:**
- **Total Rust:** 7,455 lines across 9 modules
- **Largest:** app.rs (3,289 lines) — the immediate-mode UI loop
- **Distribution:** Storage (1,581) < App (3,289) < API (682) < Tools (804) < others
- **Complexity:** Moderate
  - Async/await with tokio runtime embedded in the app struct
  - Branching conversation tree stored in SQLite with in-memory navigation
  - SSE streaming with incremental token/tool-call parsing
  - egui immediate-mode UI with 150+ fields in ChatApp
  - Schema migration on launch (v0→v2 ALTERs)

**No external Rust bindings** to C/C++ (SQLite bundled via rusqlite, font lookup via font-kit).

---

### 6. **Config Files & Assets**

**Config Layer 1 — Bundled Defaults:**
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/example.config.toml` — template, seeded on first launch to `~/.config/hchat/config.toml`

**Config Layer 2 — Per-Conversation:**
- SQLite `conversations` table columns: model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences, endpoint, working_dir

**Assets:**
- Default tool TOMLs bundled in binary at compile time (lines in `tools.rs`): read_file, list_directory, search_files, write_file, run_shell
- System fonts loaded at runtime via font-kit

**Platform-Specific Code:**
- `dirs::config_dir()` / `dirs::data_dir()` — cross-platform config/data paths (Linux ~/.config, ~/.local/share; macOS ~/Library/Preferences, ~/Library/Application Support)
- Unix-only: `fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o700)` in config.rs and storage.rs (directories created with 700 perms)
- eframe/egui handle Vulkan/OpenGL on Linux, Metal on macOS automatically

---

### 7. **Key Design Patterns**

**Async Bridging:**
- Single `tokio::runtime::Runtime` embedded in ChatApp lives for the app's lifetime
- API calls spawned as tasks (`runtime.spawn(async { ... })`)
- Results flow back via `mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>` (no sync boundary — channels live inside ChatApp)
- `process_events()` drains channels every frame; items applied to in-memory messages
- CancellationToken for stop/cancel during streaming

**Incremental Save:**
- Messages marked `id=None` are INSERT'd (rowid written back into the struct)
- Messages marked `id=Some` are UPDATE'd in place
- Parent linkage auto-linked to prior message in slice (no caller threading)
- Siblings preserved on disk; active path walked on load

**Branching Navigation:**
- Every message has `parent_id` (None = root), `branch_index` (sibling counter)
- Siblings indexed by (parent_id, branch_index, created_at DESC)
- Load path: walk from newest root, pick newest sibling at each step
- No explicit "branch" entity — just message tree with multi-parent support

**Streaming Resilience:**
- UTF-8 boundary aware byte buffering (don't split multibyte chars)
- Tool call accumulation by slot index across chunks
- Empty content/reasoning deltas skipped (provider keep-alives)
- Stream buffer cap (1MB) and error body truncation (4KB)

**Event Sourcing (Implicit):**
- Messages immutable once saved (UPDATE only on content for streaming appends)
- Presets are snapshots of ConversationSettings, cloned on apply
- Drafts persisted inline in conversations row (not a separate table)

---

### 8. **File Paths (Absolute)**

**Source:**
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/main.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/api.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/message.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/storage.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/config.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/commands.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/markdown.rs`
- `/Users/heath/code/hChat/src/tools.rs`

**Config/Data (Runtime):**
- `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — global settings
- `~/.config/hchat/tools/*.toml` — user-defined tools
- `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux) / `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) — SQLite DB

**Build Artifact:**
- `target/release/hchat` — optimized binary

---

This codebase is a well-structured Rust desktop app with good separation of concerns: UI (app.rs), networking (api.rs), persistence (storage.rs), and tooling (tools.rs). The phase-based versioning and incremental feature additions show deliberate design. Ready for a major rewrite!

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have enough comprehensive information. Let me compile the detailed research report for the user.

---

## Comprehensive Research Report: vllm-studio

Based on my investigation of the vllm-studio GitHub repository and ecosystem, here's what you need to know:

### 1. **What is vllm-studio? Purpose and Main Features**

vllm-studio is a **local-first workstation desktop application** for running, managing, and using self-hosted LLM (Large Language Model) backends. It's designed as an integrated control panel that combines:

- **Model lifecycle management** - Launch, evict, monitor, and manage inference models
- **System monitoring** - Real-time GPU metrics, logs, and runtime statistics
- **OpenAI-compatible proxy routes** - Expose your local models via standard OpenAI API endpoints (for chat, models list, completions)
- **Chat interface** - Interact directly with your models through a built-in web UI
- **Agent runtime** - Run coding agents against your models (accessible at `/agent`)
- **Remote connectivity** - Connect frontends to controllers running on remote GPU-equipped machines
- **Multi-backend support** - Works with vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, MLX, and ExLlama v3

It's meant for researchers, developers, and power users who want to self-host models locally without cloud dependencies.

---

### 2. **Tech Stack - Language, Framework, UI, Packaging**

**Frontend/Desktop:**
- **Framework**: Next.js (React-based web framework)
- **Desktop Shell**: Electron (not Tauri)
- **UI Language**: TypeScript (97.2% of the codebase is TypeScript)
- **Styling/Rendering**: Markdown support with syntax highlighting; artifact rendering system for code/HTML/SVG previews

**Backend/Controller:**
- **Runtime**: Bun (a modern JavaScript runtime, faster than Node.js)
- **Framework**: Hono (lightweight web framework built for Bun and Cloudflare Workers)
- **API Server**: Runs on port 8080

**CLI:**
- Built with Bun

**Packaging/Distribution:**
- Electron app for macOS/Linux desktop
- Monorepo structure (single npm workspace with frontend, controller, CLI subpackages)
- Docker support available (docker-compose.yml provided)
- Deployment scripts for remote servers (`scripts/deploy-remote.sh`)

**Development Requirements:**
- Node.js 20+
- npm
- Bun 1.x
- Optional: CUDA stack (NVIDIA), Python with mlx-lm (Apple Silicon), llama.cpp binary, Docker

---

### 3. **Architecture - How It Manages vLLM and Model Serving**

The architecture is **three-tiered**:

```
User Interfaces (Electron, Web UI, CLI)
    ↓
Frontend Server (Next.js routes on port 3000 for web, 3001 locally)
    ↓
Controller API (Bun + Hono on port 8080)
    ↓
Inference Runtimes (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, MLX, ExLlama v3)
```

**Process Management:**
- The controller uses **Bun's spawn API** to launch and manage model server processes
- ProcessManager maintains a registry of running processes
- Supports **lifecycle operations**: launch, evict, status, recipe management, model downloads
- Uses **SQLite** (RecipeStore) for persistent configuration of model recipes

**Backend Integration Model:**
- **NOT a unified abstraction** - vllm-studio directly spawns/manages each backend type
- For **vLLM**: Launches vLLM server processes directly
- For **SGLang**: Launches SGLang launch-servers
- For **llama.cpp**: Launches llama-server binaries (for GGUF models)
- For **MLX**: Launches MLX mlx_lm.server (Apple Silicon)
- For **ExLlama v3**: Launches with explicit command overrides

**Remote Backend Support:**
- Frontend can connect to a controller running on a **remote machine** (via environment variables: `REMOTE_HOST`, `REMOTE_USER`, `REMOTE_PATH`, `REMOTE_URL`)
- Deployed via `scripts/deploy-remote.sh`
- You can either run locally or point to a GPU-equipped remote server
- vLLM's own distributed inference (tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism) is separate and handled by vLLM itself

---

### 4. **Metrics Collection - GPU, VRAM, Temperature, Decode Tok/s, TTFT, Prefill**

This is where I have **partial visibility**. Here's what I found:

**GPU Metrics Collection Method:**
- The system **polls `/api/gpu-status` every 5 seconds**
- This endpoint **executes nvidia-smi directly** to retrieve per-device GPU statistics
- Uses a `safe_int()` helper function to handle special values (N/A) returned by nvidia-smi
- **NVIDIA Jetson devices** (Nano, Xavier, Orin) get special handling via `/proc/meminfo` for unified memory

**Metrics Available:**
- GPU memory usage (VRAM)
- GPU utilization percentage
- Temperature (if available from nvidia-smi)
- Device-level statistics

**Performance Metrics (vLLM native):**
The controller likely proxies vLLM's native Prometheus `/metrics` endpoint, which exposes:
- `vllm:num_requests_running` - Current concurrent requests
- `vllm:num_requests_waiting` - Queue depth
- `vllm:kv_cache_usage_perc` - KV cache fill rate
- `vllm:time_to_first_token_seconds` - TTFT latency
- `vllm:e2e_request_latency_seconds` - End-to-end latency
- GPU cache usage (%) - KV cache metrics

**I could NOT access the specific code** for:
- How decode tok/s (throughput) is computed/displayed
- Whether it pulls directly from vLLM's Prometheus endpoint or processes nvidia-smi output further
- Exact UI rendering of these metrics
- Whether prefill vs. decode latency is tracked separately

The architecture suggests it likely **proxies vLLM's Prometheus metrics** while also **scraping nvidia-smi for system-level GPU stats**, but I couldn't verify the exact integration points without accessing the controller service implementation files directly.

---

### 5. **Chat Interface and Markdown/Artifact Rendering**

**Chat Interface:**
- Built into the Next.js frontend
- OpenAI-compatible endpoints allow chat interactions
- Supports multi-turn conversations
- File operations are local-only and stored under `data/agentfs`

**Markdown & Artifact Rendering:**
- Messages render with **markdown support**
- **Syntax highlighting** for code blocks
- **Artifact system** for rendering HTML, SVG, and React previews separately from markdown
- Messages use consistent styling with `.chat-markdown` class
- Code blocks are excluded from standard message styling to preserve their color schemes
- Supports interactive elements in rendered content

---

### 6. **Multi-Backend and Multi-Machine Management**

**Multiple Backends (Same Machine):**
- You can have multiple recipe configurations for different models
- RecipeStore (SQLite) manages model configurations
- Controller can manage launch/evict operations across different backend types

**Multiple Machines:**
- **Local + Remote**: Frontend can connect to either a local controller (port 8080) or a remote controller (specified via `REMOTE_*` env vars)
- **Not built for multi-machine distributed serving** - Each controller manages its own set of backends
- If you need multi-GPU distributed inference, you rely on **vLLM's native distributed capabilities** (tensor parallelism + pipeline parallelism with Ray)
- Deployment is manual: you deploy the controller to a GPU machine, then point your local frontend to it

---

### 7. **MLX Support and Scope (vLLM/CUDA only?)**

**MLX Support: YES**
- **Explicitly supported** via MLX recipe plugin
- Uses `mlx_lm.server` backend for Apple Silicon machines
- MLX is a Apple framework optimized for Apple Silicon's unified memory

**vLLM/CUDA Scope:**
- **Not CUDA-only** - vllm-studio explicitly supports:
  - vLLM (can run on CUDA, Metal via vLLM-Metal plugin, or CPU)
  - SGLang (similar flexibility)
  - llama.cpp (CPU + GPU, very portable)
  - MLX (Apple Silicon dedicated)
  - ExLlama v3 (GPU-optimized but portable)

**Hardware Flexibility:**
- macOS + Apple Silicon: MLX native, or vLLM-Metal
- Linux + NVIDIA: vLLM, SGLang, ExLlama v3
- Any platform: llama.cpp (works on CPU, mobile, etc.)

The project is **hardware-agnostic** at the application level; it just spawns whatever backend you configure.

---

## Summary for Your Rewrite

If your user wants to rewrite their app in the style of vllm-studio, the key architectural patterns are:

1. **Electron + Next.js for the UI** (desktop + web)
2. **Bun + Hono for the backend controller** (lightweight, fast API server)
3. **Process spawning + registry pattern** for managing backend services
4. **SQLite for configuration persistence**
5. **Direct system integration** (nvidia-smi scraping, backend spawning)
6. **Prometheus metrics proxying** (if available from the upstream service)
7. **Remote-first connectivity** (env-var based controller discovery)
8. **Monorepo structure** with clear separation of frontend/controller/CLI

The stack is notably **TypeScript-heavy, Bun/Hono instead of traditional Node.js**, and **Electron instead of Tauri**.

---

**Sources:**
- [GitHub - sybil-solutions/vllm-studio](https://github.com/sybil-solutions/vllm-studio)
- [vllm-studio/README.md](https://github.com/sybil-solutions/vllm-studio/blob/main/README.md)
- [vllm-studio/AGENTS.md](https://github.com/sybil-solutions/vllm-studio/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [Metrics - vLLM Documentation](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/stable/design/metrics/)
- [Production Metrics - vLLM Documentation](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/stable/usage/metrics/)
- [Monitor LLM Inference in Production (2026): Prometheus & Grafana for vLLM, TGI, llama.cpp](https://www.glukhov.org/observability/monitoring-llm-inference-prometheus-grafana/)
- [How to Monitor GPU, CPU, and Memory Usage of a vLLM Server Using Prometheus, DCGM Exporter, cAdvisor, and Grafana](https://medium.com/@kimdoil1211/how-to-monitor-gpu-cpu-and-memory-usage-of-a-vllm-server-using-prometheus-dcgm-exporter-f988afd70460)
- [Local GPU Monitoring For LLMs With Prometheus And Grafana](https://xebia.com/blog/local-deployment-of-prometheus-and-grafana/)
- [Distributed Inference and Serving - vLLM Documentation](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/v0.8.0/serving/distributed_serving.html)
- [Two paths to vLLM on Apple Silicon - vllm-metal vs vllm-mlx](https://blog.labs.purplemaia.org/two-paths-to-vllm-on-apple-silicon-vllm-metal-vs-vllm-mlx/)
- [GitHub - waybarrios/vllm-mlx: OpenAI and Anthropic compatible server for Apple Silicon](https://github.com/waybarrios/vllm-mlx)
- [Local LLM Hosting: Complete 2025 Guide — Ollama, vLLM, LocalAI, Jan, LM Studio & More](https://medium.com/@rosgluk/local-llm-hosting-complete-2025-guide-ollama-vllm-localai-jan-lm-studio-more-f98136ce7e4a)

- **Framework**: Hono (lightweight web framework built for Bun and Cloudflare Workers)
- **API Server**: Runs on port 8080

**CLI:**
- Built with Bun

**Packaging/Distribution:**
- Electron app for macOS/Linux desktop
- Monorepo structure (single npm workspace with frontend, controller, CLI subpackages)
- Docker support available (docker-compose.yml provided)
- Deployment scripts for remote servers (`scripts/deploy-remote.sh`)

**Development Requirements:**
- Node.js 20+
- npm
- Bun 1.x
- Optional: CUDA stack (NVIDIA), Python with mlx-lm (Apple Silicon), llama.cpp binary, Docker

---

### 3. **Architecture - How It Manages vLLM and Model Serving**

The architecture is **three-tiered**:

```
User Interfaces (Electron, Web UI, CLI)
    ↓
Frontend Server (Next.js routes on port 3000 for web, 3001 locally)
    ↓
Controller API (Bun + Hono on port 8080)
    ↓
Inference Runtimes (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, MLX, ExLlama v3)
```

**Process Management:**
- The controller uses **Bun's spawn API** to launch and manage model server processes
- ProcessManager maintains a registry of running processes
- Supports **lifecycle operations**: launch, evict, status, recipe management, model downloads
- Uses **SQLite** (RecipeStore) for persistent configuration of model recipes

**Backend Integration Model:**
- **NOT a unified abstraction** - vllm-studio directly spawns/manages each backend type
- For **vLLM**: Launches vLLM server processes directly
- For **SGLang**: Launches SGLang launch-servers
- For **llama.cpp**: Launches llama-server binaries (for GGUF models)
- For **MLX**: Launches MLX mlx_lm.server (Apple Silicon)
- For **ExLlama v3**: Launches with explicit command overrides

**Remote Backend Support:**
- Frontend can connect to a controller running on a **remote machine** (via environment variables: `REMOTE_HOST`, `REMOTE_USER`, `REMOTE_PATH`, `REMOTE_URL`)
- Deployed via `scripts/deploy-remote.sh`
- You can either run locally or point to a GPU-equipped remote server
- vLLM's own distributed inference (tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism) is separate and handled by vLLM itself

---

### 4. **Metrics Collection - GPU, VRAM, Temperature, Decode Tok/s, TTFT, Prefill**

This is where I have **partial visibility**. Here's what I found:

**GPU Metrics Collection Method:**
- The system **polls `/api/gpu-status` every 5 seconds**
- This endpoint **executes nvidia-smi directly** to retrieve per-device GPU statistics
- Uses a `safe_int()` helper function to handle special values (N/A) returned by nvidia-smi
- **NVIDIA Jetson devices** (Nano, Xavier, Orin) get special handling via `/proc/meminfo` for unified memory

**Metrics Available:**
- GPU memory usage (VRAM)
- GPU utilization percentage
- Temperature (if available from nvidia-smi)
- Device-level statistics

**Performance Metrics (vLLM native):**
The controller likely proxies vLLM's native Prometheus `/metrics` endpoint, which exposes:
- `vllm:num_requests_running` - Current concurrent requests
- `vllm:num_requests_waiting` - Queue depth
- `vllm:kv_cache_usage_perc` - KV cache fill rate
- `vllm:time_to_first_token_seconds` - TTFT latency
- `vllm:e2e_request_latency_seconds` - End-to-end latency
- GPU cache usage (%) - KV cache metrics

**I could NOT access the specific code** for:
- How decode tok/s (throughput) is computed/displayed
- Whether it pulls directly from vLLM's Prometheus endpoint or processes nvidia-smi output further
- Exact UI rendering of these metrics
- Whether prefill vs. decode latency is tracked separately

The architecture suggests it likely **proxies vLLM's Prometheus metrics** while also **scraping nvidia-smi for system-level GPU stats**, but I couldn't verify the exact integration points without accessing the controller service implementation files directly.

---

### 5. **Chat Interface and Markdown/Artifact Rendering**

**Chat Interface:**
- Built into the Next.js frontend
- OpenAI-compatible endpoints allow chat interactions
- Supports multi-turn conversations
- File operations are local-only and stored under `data/agentfs`

**Markdown & Artifact Rendering:**
- Messages render with **markdown support**
- **Syntax highlighting** for code blocks
- **Artifact system** for rendering HTML, SVG, and React previews separately from markdown
- Messages use consistent styling with `.chat-markdown` class
- Code blocks are excluded from standard message styling to preserve their color schemes
- Supports interactive elements in rendered content

---

### 6. **Multi-Backend and Multi-Machine Management**

**Multiple Backends (Same Machine):**
- You can have multiple recipe configurations for different models
- RecipeStore (SQLite) manages model configurations
- Controller can manage launch/evict operations across different backend types

**Multiple Machines:**
- **Local + Remote**: Frontend can connect to either a local controller (port 8080) or a remote controller (specified via `REMOTE_*` env vars)
- **Not built for multi-machine distributed serving** - Each controller manages its own set of backends
- If you need multi-GPU distributed inference, you rely on **vLLM's native distributed capabilities** (tensor parallelism + pipeline parallelism with Ray)
- Deployment is manual: you deploy the controller to a GPU machine, then point your local frontend to it

---

### 7. **MLX Support and Scope (vLLM/CUDA only?)**

**MLX Support: YES**
- **Explicitly supported** via MLX recipe plugin
- Uses `mlx_lm.server` backend for Apple Silicon machines
- MLX is a Apple framework optimized for Apple Silicon's unified memory

**vLLM/CUDA Scope:**
- **Not CUDA-only** - vllm-studio explicitly supports:
  - vLLM (can run on CUDA, Metal via vLLM-Metal plugin, or CPU)
  - SGLang (similar flexibility)
  - llama.cpp (CPU + GPU, very portable)
  - MLX (Apple Silicon dedicated)
  - ExLlama v3 (GPU-optimized but portable)

**Hardware Flexibility:**
- macOS + Apple Silicon: MLX native, or vLLM-Metal
- Linux + NVIDIA: vLLM, SGLang, ExLlama v3
- Any platform: llama.cpp (works on CPU, mobile, etc.)

The project is **hardware-agnostic** at the application level; it just spawns whatever backend you configure.

---

## Summary for Your Rewrite

If your user wants to rewrite their app in the style of vllm-studio, the key architectural patterns are:

1. **Electron + Next.js for the UI** (desktop + web)
2. **Bun + Hono for the backend controller** (lightweight, fast API server)
3. **Process spawning + registry pattern** for managing backend services
4. **SQLite for configuration persistence**
5. **Direct system integration** (nvidia-smi scraping, backend spawning)
6. **Prometheus metrics proxying** (if available from the upstream service)
7. **Remote-first connectivity** (env-var based controller discovery)
8. **Monorepo structure** with clear separation of frontend/controller/CLI

The stack is notably **TypeScript-heavy, Bun/Hono instead of traditional Node.js**, and **Electron instead of Tauri**.

**Current hChat:** ~7,500 lines of solid Rust/egui — working chat, SSE streaming, SQLite history with branching, tools, presets, markdown rendering. The "small fast binary" ethos.

**vllm-studio:** Electron + Next.js frontend, Bun/Hono controller that *spawns and manages* vLLM/SGLang/llama.cpp/MLX processes, scrapes `nvidia-smi` every 5s + proxies vLLM's Prometheus `/metrics`, and renders artifacts. A much heavier, server-management-oriented stack.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the macmon Rust crate:

## Overview

Macmon is an Apple Silicon system monitor and Rust library for CPU/GPU power, temperature, and RAM monitoring that requires no sudo. It provides a TUI, JSON/Prometheus metrics server, and Rust library.

## How It Works with IOReport

macOS has an IOReport shared library that returns hardware information and can perform sampling to return CPU/GPU and energy usage. Macmon uses a private macOS API to gather similar metrics (essentially the same as powermetrics), but runs without sudo.

The implementation uses several IOReport functions: to subscribe to a channel, it's necessary to get the internal channel struct as CFDictionary using IOReportCopyChannelsInGroup and merge all the dicts using IOReportMergeChannels.

## Features and Usage

It displays real-time CPU, GPU and Apple Neural Engine (ANE) power usage, along with CPU utilization, RAM/Swap usage and temperature information in the terminal. The tool supports specifying the number of samples using the -s or --samples parameter, and setting the update interval in milliseconds using the -i or --interval parameter.

The crate can be added to projects with `cargo add macmon` and used as a library to collect Apple Silicon metrics in custom applications.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the llama.cpp llama-server Prometheus metrics endpoint:

## Metrics Endpoint Overview

The llama.cpp server supports a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint that must be enabled with a flag (e.g., --metrics). The /metrics endpoint exposes Prometheus-formatted performance counters and gauges.

## Key Metrics Available

The /metrics endpoint tracks several important metrics:

**Prompt and Prediction Tokens:**
- llamacpp_prompt_tokens_total - Number of prompt tokens processed
- llamacpp_tokens_predicted_total - Number of generation tokens processed

**Throughput Metrics:**
- prompt_tokens_seconds - Average prompt throughput in tokens/s
- llamacpp:predicted_tokens_seconds - Generation Speed (tokens/sec)

## Configuration

To enable the metrics endpoint, use the `--metrics` flag when starting llama-server. Prometheus metrics require the --metrics flag to be enabled.

The metrics can be accessed at the standard Prometheus endpoint and are formatted in Prometheus text exposition format for easy integration with Prometheus and Grafana monitoring stacks.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about oMLX:

## Overview

oMLX is a native macOS inference server built on MLX with paged SSD KV caching for Apple Silicon. It provides a drop-in replacement for OpenAI and Anthropic APIs, supporting streaming usage stats and vision inputs.

## Launch Commands

The basic launch command for oMLX is:

omlx serve --model-dir ~/models

You can also customize it with various options:
- Choose a memory guard tier at startup with --memory-guard safe, set a custom memory guard ceiling in GB with --memory-guard-gb, enable SSD cache for KV blocks with --paged-ssd-cache-dir, adjust hot cache size, and adjust max concurrent requests

## Default Port

The default oMLX port is 8000. You can verify the server is running by checking `http://localhost:8000/v1/models`.

## API Compatibility

oMLX provides both OpenAI-compatible (/v1/chat/completions) and Anthropic-compatible (/v1/messages) API endpoints, working as a drop-in backend for all three.

## Installation

Installation is available via Homebrew, with commands like `omlx start`, `omlx stop`, and `omlx restart` for managing the server as a background service.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about NVIDIA DGX Spark GB10 and the topics you mentioned:

## DGX Spark GB10 Overview

DGX Spark is a personal AI supercomputer that's powered by the NVIDIA GB10 Superchip. GB10 is a multi-die SoC co-designed by NVIDIA and MediaTek. A TSMC 3nm ARM CPU die (ten Cortex-X925 cores plus ten Cortex-A725 cores) connects to a Blackwell GPU die via NVLink C2C, and 128GB of LPDDR5X memory is shared between the CPU and GPU at 273 GB/s bandwidth.

## Unified Memory

DGX Spark systems use a unified memory architecture (UMA), where the GPU shares system memory (DRAM) with the CPU and other compute engines. This design reduces latency and allows larger amounts of memory to be used for GPU workloads.

## Power and Temperature

The entire GB10 SoC runs at 140W. Regarding temperatures, the exhaust temperature can reach at least 60°C under load.

## nvidia-smi Limitations

There are important limitations with nvidia-smi on DGX Spark due to its unified memory architecture. nvidia-smi reports "Not Supported" for memory usage on this platform (Unified Memory architecture). Additionally, fan speed is likely managed by the chassis controller or SoC thermal subsystem, not exposed via standard NVML fan APIs for discrete cards.

However, power usage correctly shows wattage (e.g., 58 W).

input.content:
# Rewrite hChat → Tauri LLM workstation (chat + metrics dashboard + artifacts)

## Context

hChat today is a Rust/egui desktop chat client (~7,500 LOC) for OpenAI-compatible
local models. It works well but is a pure chat client with no insight into the
inference servers behind it, and its egui UI can't easily match the polished,
terminal-minimal look of vllm-studio or render artifacts like Claude Desktop.

You want to use it against **two backends at once** — a remote **DGX Spark**
(GB10, vLLM/CUDA) and your **MacBook Pro** running **oMLX** (MLX models, port 8000)
and **llama.cpp** (GGUF) — and you want live insight into **decode tok/s, TTFT,
prefill t/s, requests, VRAM, power, GPU util, temp**, plus a **left artifacts
sidebar** that renders the markdown/code/HTML your models produce.

**Decisions locked in (from Q&A):**
- **Stack: Tauri** — keep the Rust core (chat, streaming, SQLite, tools, config),
  replace the egui UI with a React/TS web frontend. ~15–25 MB binary, native perf.
- **Scope: connect-only first** — point at already-running servers; show dashboard +
  chat + artifacts. Process spawn/evict ("Stop"/MODELS dropdown) is a later phase.
- **Local GGUF: llama.cpp**; **local MLX: oMLX**. Both run side-by-side on the Mac.
- **Spark metrics: small agent (recommended below)** — see Metrics section.

## Target architecture

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Frontend  src/  — React + TypeScript + Vite    │
│   • Status dashboard (uPlot charts)            │
│   • Chat view (markdown, branching, tools)     │
│   • Artifacts sidebar (react-markdown + shiki) │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↕ Tauri commands (invoke) + events (emit)
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Rust core  src-tauri/src/                      │
│   core/api.rs        ← reuse (streaming, SSE)  │
│   core/message.rs    ← reuse as-is             │
│   core/storage.rs    ← reuse as-is (SQLite)    │
│   core/config.rs     ← reuse + extend (backends)│
│   core/commands.rs   ← reuse (slash cmds)      │
│   core/markdown.rs   ← reuse (segmenting)      │
│   core/tools.rs      ← reuse (tool exec)       │
│   metrics/*          ← NEW (scrape + gpu)      │
│   commands.rs        ← NEW Tauri command layer │
│   lib.rs / main.rs   ← NEW Tauri setup + state │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Reuse map (~70% of existing Rust survives):**
- `src/api.rs`, `message.rs`, `storage.rs`, `config.rs`, `commands.rs`,
  `markdown.rs`, `tools.rs` → move under `src-tauri/src/core/` largely unchanged.
- `src/app.rs` (3,289 lines of egui) and `src/main.rs` → **discarded**, replaced by
  a Tauri command layer + a Rust `AppState` (the non-UI fields of today's `ChatApp`:
  `Storage`, current conversation, loaded tools, saved backends, etc.).
- Streaming changes shape only: instead of `mpsc` drained per egui frame
  (`process_events`), `stream_chat`'s `StreamEvent`s are forwarded to the frontend
  via a Tauri `Channel`/`emit` keyed by a request id. The SSE parser in `api.rs` is
  unchanged.

## New: backends model (config.rs extension)

Generalize today's `Endpoint { url, api_key }` into a **Backend**:

```
Backend {
  id, name,                       // "spark", "mac-omlx", "mac-llamacpp"
  base_url, api_key,              // OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint
  runtime: Vllm | Omlx | LlamaCpp,
  prometheus_url: Option<String>, // /metrics scrape (vLLM, llama.cpp --metrics)
  gpu_source: None | Macmon | SparkAgent{url} | Ssh{host},
}
```

Stored in `config.toml` (extend existing `saved_endpoints`). Seed two examples:
Spark (vLLM + SparkAgent) and Mac (oMLX + macmon; llama.cpp + macmon).

## New: metrics subsystem (`src-tauri/src/metrics/`)

Three independent collectors feed a shared `MetricsState`; a poller task emits a
snapshot to the frontend every ~1 s via Tauri events. Charts keep a rolling
~30-min ring buffer (mirrors the screenshot's "LAST 30 MINUTES").

1. **Client-measured per-request** (`metrics/request.rs`) — works for *every*
   backend incl. oMLX, no server support needed. Wrap our own `stream_chat`:
   - **TTFT** = first-token time − request-send time.
   - **decode tok/s** = completion_tokens / (done − first_token).
   - **prefill t/s** = prompt_tokens / TTFT.
   These populate the big tiles for the active conversation's last request.

2. **Server scrape** (`metrics/prometheus.rs`) — poll `prometheus_url`, parse the
   Prometheus text format (small hand-rolled parser; no heavy dep). Map:
   - vLLM: `vllm:generation_tokens_total` & `vllm:prompt_tokens_total` (rate →
     decode/prefill tok/s), `vllm:time_to_first_token_seconds` (histogram → TTFT),
     `vllm:num_requests_running`/`_waiting`, `vllm:gpu_cache_usage_perc` (KV).
   - llama.cpp (`--metrics`): `llamacpp:predicted_tokens_seconds`,
     `llamacpp:prompt_tokens_seconds`, `*_total` counters.
   Gives server-wide view (incl. requests not from this app) + the totals row.

3. **GPU / system** (`metrics/gpu.rs`):
   - **Mac (local):** `macmon` crate (`cargo add macmon`, no sudo) → GPU power (W),
     GPU util/residency, temp, unified memory. Drives VRAM/POWER/TEMP tiles locally.
   - **Spark (remote):** **recommended — ship a tiny `hchat-agent` binary** (second
     `[[bin]]` target in the workspace, reuses our Rust). It runs
     `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=power.draw,power.limit,temperature.gpu,utilization.gpu
     --format=csv,noheader,nounits` **plus reads `/proc/meminfo`** and serves JSON
     over HTTP. **Why both:** on GB10/DGX Spark `nvidia-smi` returns power ✓ and
     temp ✓ but **memory = "Not Supported"** (unified memory), so VRAM must come from
     `/proc/meminfo` (MemTotal/MemAvailable) — same approach vllm-studio uses for
     Jetson. One static ARM64 binary you `scp` to the Spark and run; low latency,
     correct VRAM. **Fallback** (zero install): `gpu_source: Ssh{host}` runs the same
     two commands over SSH instead. (This agent is just a metrics exporter — it does
     **not** manage inference servers, so it stays within "connect-only first".)

**Recommendation for your Spark question (vram + power + temp):** use the
`hchat-agent` sidecar. It's the only option that gets you *all three* on GB10,
because plain `nvidia-smi` can't report unified-memory VRAM and vLLM `/metrics`
has no GPU power/temp at all.

## New: frontend (`src/`, React + TS + Vite)

Layout mirrors the screenshot + Claude Desktop:
- **Left rail:** Workspace nav (Status · Usage · Models · Server), Projects/
  conversations list (reusing `storage.rs` conversation list, search, pin, rename,
  branching), Settings. Terminal-minimal dark theme, monospace numerics.
- **Status view:** ACTIVE bar (runtime/badge/port, Stop disabled for now, MODELS,
  LOGS, BENCH), big tiles **DECODE / TTFT / PREFILL** with peak sub-labels, **REQ /
  VRAM / POWER** tiles, totals row (total/prompt/completion tokens, duration), two
  time-series charts **THROUGHPUT (tok/s)** and **TTFT (ms) & REQUESTS**, a **GPUS**
  list (per-GPU util/mem/temp/power bars), and a **CONTROLLER LOGS** tail.
- **Chat view:** reuse streaming/markdown/branching/tools/presets/attachments. Code
  blocks, `<think>` reasoning collapse, copy buttons — port the behavior, restyle.
- **Artifacts sidebar (left, collapsible):** detect fenced code / HTML / SVG /
  mermaid in assistant messages and render them as selectable artifacts;
  `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `shiki` for rendering. Click a code block in
  chat → opens in the artifact panel, like Claude Desktop.

Charts: **uPlot** (tiny, fast, fits the minimal aesthetic). Markdown: react-markdown
+ shiki. No component framework lock-in; hand-styled CSS to match the screenshot.

## Build phases (execution order)

- **A — Tauri scaffold + core port.** `npm create tauri-app` (React/TS/Vite); move
  the 7 reusable Rust modules under `core/`; build the Tauri command layer
  (`list_conversations`, `load_conversation`, `send_message` w/ streaming channel,
  `fetch_models`, settings/presets/tools, slash commands). Result: chat works
  end-to-end in the web UI with SQLite history, branching, streaming, tools — feature
  parity with today, new shell.
- **B — Metrics dashboard.** Backends config; the three collectors; poller→events;
  Status view with tiles, charts, GPU list, logs tail. Includes the `hchat-agent`
  bin + a one-liner deploy note for the Spark.
- **C — Artifacts sidebar.** Artifact detection + rendering panel.
- **D — (later, out of scope now)** process spawn/evict/recipes (the real "manage"
  scope) — design left open; backends model already leaves room for it.

## Things to verify during implementation

- Confirm whether **oMLX** exposes a Prometheus `/metrics` (docs show streaming usage
  stats but not a scrape endpoint). If not, oMLX relies on client-measured metrics
  only (collector #1) — already the plan's assumption.
- Pin the **macmon** library API (it ships a Rust lib + Prometheus server; confirm the
  sampler call returns GPU power/temp/mem in the fields we need).
- Confirm **llama.cpp** build has `--metrics`; confirm exact metric names on the
  installed version (names have churned across releases).
- DGX Spark: confirm `utilization.gpu` is populated (some GB10 NVML fields read
  "Not Supported"); if so, derive util from the agent or omit gracefully.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. `cargo build` (workspace: app + `hchat-agent`) and `npm run tauri dev` launch the app.
2. **Chat parity:** create a conversation against the Mac oMLX endpoint, stream a
   reply, branch/regenerate, run a tool, reload — history persists (same `hchat.db`).
3. **Local metrics:** with oMLX or llama.cpp running, Status shows live DECODE/TTFT/
   PREFILL from a sent message, and VRAM/POWER/TEMP from macmon; charts fill over time.
4. **Spark metrics:** run `hchat-agent` on the Spark, point the Spark backend at it +
   vLLM `/metrics`; Status shows GPU power/temp/util + unified-memory VRAM and
   server-side throughput/requests; CONTROLLER LOGS tails.
5. **Artifacts:** ask a model for an HTML/SVG/code artifact → it renders in the left
   sidebar; clicking a chat code block opens it there.
6. `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; run the review subagent + PR ritual per
   delivery standards before merging.

```
│   core/message.rs    ← reuse as-is             │
│   core/storage.rs    ← reuse as-is (SQLite)    │
│   core/config.rs     ← reuse + extend (backends)│
│   core/commands.rs   ← reuse (slash cmds)      │
│   core/markdown.rs   ← reuse (segmenting)      │
│   core/tools.rs      ← reuse (tool exec)       │
```

## New: backends model (config.rs extension)

**`config.toml` stays the source of truth** and remains hand-editable; the existing
load/save/sanitize path in `config.rs` is kept (TOML, range-clamping, broken-file
backup). Endpoints stay **fully customizable** — arbitrary URLs/ports, per-endpoint
API keys, add/remove from the UI *and* by editing the file. Generalize today's
`Endpoint { url, api_key }` into a **Backend** (backward-compatible: a bare
`{url, api_key}` still parses, defaulting `runtime = OpenAi`, no metrics):

```
Backend {
  id, name,                       // "spark", "mac-omlx", "openrouter"
  base_url, api_key,              // OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint (any host/port)
  runtime: Vllm | Omlx | LlamaCpp | OpenAi,   // OpenAi = generic/cloud
  prometheus_url: Option<String>, // /metrics scrape (vLLM, llama.cpp --metrics)
  gpu_source: None | Macmon | SparkAgent{url} | Ssh{host},
}
```

Seed examples covering all three of your real backends:
- **Spark** — remote vLLM, `gpu_source = SparkAgent`, `prometheus_url` set.
- **mac-omlx** — `base_url = http://localhost:42069/v1` (your custom oMLX port, **not**
  the 8000 default), `runtime = Omlx`, `gpu_source = Macmon`, no prometheus (oMLX
  metrics come from client-measured per-request).
- **mac-llamacpp** — local GGUF, `runtime = LlamaCpp`, `gpu_source = Macmon`,
  `prometheus_url` set (`--metrics`).
- **openrouter** — `runtime = OpenAi`, `gpu_source = None`, no prometheus; cloud
  chat works exactly as today (OpenRouter URL auto-fix logic in `api.rs` is kept).

**Graceful degradation by backend:** the dashboard renders whatever a backend
provides. Cloud/OpenAI backends (OpenRouter) show **only** client-measured tiles
(TTFT, decode, prefill, tokens, cost) and hide the GPU/server-scrape sections rather
than showing empty boxes. So OpenRouter remains a normal, fully usable chat endpoint.
