input.plan:
# Rewrite hChat → Tauri LLM workstation (chat + metrics dashboard + artifacts)

## Context

hChat today is a Rust/egui desktop chat client (~7,500 LOC) for OpenAI-compatible
local models. It works well but is a pure chat client with no insight into the
inference servers behind it, and its egui UI can't easily match the polished,
terminal-minimal look of vllm-studio or render artifacts like Claude Desktop.

You want to use it against **two backends at once** — a remote **DGX Spark**
(GB10, vLLM/CUDA) and your **MacBook Pro** running **oMLX** (MLX models, port 8000)
and **llama.cpp** (GGUF) — and you want live insight into **decode tok/s, TTFT,
prefill t/s, requests, VRAM, power, GPU util, temp**, plus a **left artifacts
sidebar** that renders the markdown/code/HTML your models produce.

**Decisions locked in (from Q&A):**
- **Stack: Tauri** — keep the Rust core (chat, streaming, SQLite, tools, config),
  replace the egui UI with a React/TS web frontend. ~15–25 MB binary, native perf.
- **Scope: connect-only first** — point at already-running servers; show dashboard +
  chat + artifacts. Process spawn/evict ("Stop"/MODELS dropdown) is a later phase.
- **Local GGUF: llama.cpp**; **local MLX: oMLX**. Both run side-by-side on the Mac.
- **Spark metrics: small agent (recommended below)** — see Metrics section.

## Target architecture

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Frontend  src/  — React + TypeScript + Vite    │
│   • Status dashboard (uPlot charts)            │
│   • Chat view (markdown, branching, tools)     │
│   • Artifacts sidebar (react-markdown + shiki) │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↕ Tauri commands (invoke) + events (emit)
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Rust core  src-tauri/src/                      │
│   core/api.rs        ← reuse (streaming, SSE)  │
│   core/message.rs    ← reuse as-is             │
│   core/storage.rs    ← reuse as-is (SQLite)    │
│   core/config.rs     ← reuse + extend (backends)│
│   core/commands.rs   ← reuse (slash cmds)      │
│   core/markdown.rs   ← reuse (segmenting)      │
│   core/tools.rs      ← reuse (tool exec)       │
│   metrics/*          ← NEW (scrape + gpu)      │
│   commands.rs        ← NEW Tauri command layer │
│   lib.rs / main.rs   ← NEW Tauri setup + state │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Reuse map (~70% of existing Rust survives):**
- `src/api.rs`, `message.rs`, `storage.rs`, `config.rs`, `commands.rs`,
  `markdown.rs`, `tools.rs` → move under `src-tauri/src/core/` largely unchanged.
- `src/app.rs` (3,289 lines of egui) and `src/main.rs` → **discarded**, replaced by
  a Tauri command layer + a Rust `AppState` (the non-UI fields of today's `ChatApp`:
  `Storage`, current conversation, loaded tools, saved backends, etc.).
- Streaming changes shape only: instead of `mpsc` drained per egui frame
  (`process_events`), `stream_chat`'s `StreamEvent`s are forwarded to the frontend
  via a Tauri `Channel`/`emit` keyed by a request id. The SSE parser in `api.rs` is
  unchanged.

## New: backends model (config.rs extension)

**`config.toml` stays the source of truth** and remains hand-editable; the existing
load/save/sanitize path in `config.rs` is kept (TOML, range-clamping, broken-file
backup). Endpoints stay **fully customizable** — arbitrary URLs/ports, per-endpoint
API keys, add/remove from the UI *and* by editing the file. Generalize today's
`Endpoint { url, api_key }` into a **Backend** (backward-compatible: a bare
`{url, api_key}` still parses, defaulting `runtime = OpenAi`, no metrics):

```
Backend {
  id, name,                       // "spark", "mac-omlx", "openrouter"
  base_url, api_key,              // OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint (any host/port)
  runtime: Vllm | Omlx | LlamaCpp | OpenAi,   // OpenAi = generic/cloud
  prometheus_url: Option<String>, // /metrics scrape (vLLM, llama.cpp --metrics)
  gpu_source: None | Macmon | SparkAgent{url} | Ssh{host},
}
```

Seed examples covering all three of your real backends:
- **Spark** — remote vLLM, `gpu_source = SparkAgent`, `prometheus_url` set.
- **mac-omlx** — `base_url = http://localhost:42069/v1` (your custom oMLX port, **not**
  the 8000 default), `runtime = Omlx`, `gpu_source = Macmon`, no prometheus (oMLX
  metrics come from client-measured per-request).
- **mac-llamacpp** — local GGUF, `runtime = LlamaCpp`, `gpu_source = Macmon`,
  `prometheus_url` set (`--metrics`).
- **openrouter** — `runtime = OpenAi`, `gpu_source = None`, no prometheus; cloud
  chat works exactly as today (OpenRouter URL auto-fix logic in `api.rs` is kept).

**Graceful degradation by backend:** the dashboard renders whatever a backend
provides. Cloud/OpenAI backends (OpenRouter) show **only** client-measured tiles
(TTFT, decode, prefill, tokens, cost) and hide the GPU/server-scrape sections rather
than showing empty boxes. So OpenRouter remains a normal, fully usable chat endpoint.

## New: metrics subsystem (`src-tauri/src/metrics/`)

Three independent collectors feed a shared `MetricsState`; a poller task emits a
snapshot to the frontend every ~1 s via Tauri events. Charts keep a rolling
~30-min ring buffer (mirrors the screenshot's "LAST 30 MINUTES").

1. **Client-measured per-request** (`metrics/request.rs`) — works for *every*
   backend incl. oMLX, no server support needed. Wrap our own `stream_chat`:
   - **TTFT** = first-token time − request-send time.
   - **decode tok/s** = completion_tokens / (done − first_token).
   - **prefill t/s** = prompt_tokens / TTFT.
   These populate the big tiles for the active conversation's last request.

2. **Server scrape** (`metrics/prometheus.rs`) — poll `prometheus_url`, parse the
   Prometheus text format (small hand-rolled parser; no heavy dep). Map:
   - vLLM: `vllm:generation_tokens_total` & `vllm:prompt_tokens_total` (rate →
     decode/prefill tok/s), `vllm:time_to_first_token_seconds` (histogram → TTFT),
     `vllm:num_requests_running`/`_waiting`, `vllm:gpu_cache_usage_perc` (KV).
   - llama.cpp (`--metrics`): `llamacpp:predicted_tokens_seconds`,
     `llamacpp:prompt_tokens_seconds`, `*_total` counters.
   Gives server-wide view (incl. requests not from this app) + the totals row.

3. **GPU / system** (`metrics/gpu.rs`):
   - **Mac (local):** `macmon` crate (`cargo add macmon`, no sudo) → GPU power (W),
     GPU util/residency, temp, unified memory. Drives VRAM/POWER/TEMP tiles locally.
   - **Spark (remote):** **recommended — ship a tiny `hchat-agent` binary** (second
     `[[bin]]` target in the workspace, reuses our Rust). It runs
     `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=power.draw,power.limit,temperature.gpu,utilization.gpu
     --format=csv,noheader,nounits` **plus reads `/proc/meminfo`** and serves JSON
     over HTTP. **Why both:** on GB10/DGX Spark `nvidia-smi` returns power ✓ and
     temp ✓ but **memory = "Not Supported"** (unified memory), so VRAM must come from
     `/proc/meminfo` (MemTotal/MemAvailable) — same approach vllm-studio uses for
     Jetson. One static ARM64 binary you `scp` to the Spark and run; low latency,
     correct VRAM. **Fallback** (zero install): `gpu_source: Ssh{host}` runs the same
     two commands over SSH instead. (This agent is just a metrics exporter — it does
     **not** manage inference servers, so it stays within "connect-only first".)

**Recommendation for your Spark question (vram + power + temp):** use the
`hchat-agent` sidecar. It's the only option that gets you *all three* on GB10,
because plain `nvidia-smi` can't report unified-memory VRAM and vLLM `/metrics`
has no GPU power/temp at all.

## New: frontend (`src/`, React + TS + Vite)

Layout mirrors the screenshot + Claude Desktop:
- **Left rail:** Workspace nav (Status · Usage · Models · Server), Projects/
  conversations list (reusing `storage.rs` conversation list, search, pin, rename,
  branching), Settings. Terminal-minimal dark theme, monospace numerics.
- **Status view:** ACTIVE bar (runtime/badge/port, Stop disabled for now, MODELS,
  LOGS, BENCH), big tiles **DECODE / TTFT / PREFILL** with peak sub-labels, **REQ /
  VRAM / POWER** tiles, totals row (total/prompt/completion tokens, duration), two
  time-series charts **THROUGHPUT (tok/s)** and **TTFT (ms) & REQUESTS**, a **GPUS**
  list (per-GPU util/mem/temp/power bars), and a **CONTROLLER LOGS** tail.
- **Chat view:** reuse streaming/markdown/branching/tools/presets/attachments. Code
  blocks, `<think>` reasoning collapse, copy buttons — port the behavior, restyle.
- **Artifacts sidebar (left, collapsible):** detect fenced code / HTML / SVG /
  mermaid in assistant messages and render them as selectable artifacts;
  `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `shiki` for rendering. Click a code block in
  chat → opens in the artifact panel, like Claude Desktop.

Charts: **uPlot** (tiny, fast, fits the minimal aesthetic). Markdown: react-markdown
+ shiki. No component framework lock-in; hand-styled CSS to match the screenshot.

## Build phases (execution order)

- **A — Tauri scaffold + core port.** `npm create tauri-app` (React/TS/Vite); move
  the 7 reusable Rust modules under `core/`; build the Tauri command layer
  (`list_conversations`, `load_conversation`, `send_message` w/ streaming channel,
  `fetch_models`, settings/presets/tools, slash commands). Result: chat works
  end-to-end in the web UI with SQLite history, branching, streaming, tools — feature
  parity with today, new shell.
- **B — Metrics dashboard.** Backends config; the three collectors; poller→events;
  Status view with tiles, charts, GPU list, logs tail. Includes the `hchat-agent`
  bin + a one-liner deploy note for the Spark.
- **C — Artifacts sidebar.** Artifact detection + rendering panel.
- **D — (later, out of scope now)** process spawn/evict/recipes (the real "manage"
  scope) — design left open; backends model already leaves room for it.

## Things to verify during implementation

- Confirm whether **oMLX** exposes a Prometheus `/metrics` (docs show streaming usage
  stats but not a scrape endpoint). If not, oMLX relies on client-measured metrics
  only (collector #1) — already the plan's assumption.
- Pin the **macmon** library API (it ships a Rust lib + Prometheus server; confirm the
  sampler call returns GPU power/temp/mem in the fields we need).
- Confirm **llama.cpp** build has `--metrics`; confirm exact metric names on the
  installed version (names have churned across releases).
- DGX Spark: confirm `utilization.gpu` is populated (some GB10 NVML fields read
  "Not Supported"); if so, derive util from the agent or omit gracefully.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. `cargo build` (workspace: app + `hchat-agent`) and `npm run tauri dev` launch the app.
2. **Chat parity:** create a conversation against the Mac oMLX endpoint, stream a
   reply, branch/regenerate, run a tool, reload — history persists (same `hchat.db`).
3. **Local metrics:** with oMLX or llama.cpp running, Status shows live DECODE/TTFT/
   PREFILL from a sent message, and VRAM/POWER/TEMP from macmon; charts fill over time.
4. **Spark metrics:** run `hchat-agent` on the Spark, point the Spark backend at it +
   vLLM `/metrics`; Status shows GPU power/temp/util + unified-memory VRAM and
   server-side throughput/requests; CONTROLLER LOGS tails.
5. **Artifacts:** ask a model for an HTML/SVG/code artifact → it renders in the left
   sidebar; clicking a chat code block opens it there.
6. `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; run the review subagent + PR ritual per
   delivery standards before merging.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Rewrite hChat → Tauri LLM workstation (chat + metrics dashboard + artifacts)

## Context

hChat today is a Rust/egui desktop chat client (~7,500 LOC) for OpenAI-compatible
local models. It works well but is a pure chat client with no insight into the
inference servers behind it, and its egui UI can't easily match the polished,
terminal-minimal look of vllm-studio or render artifacts like Claude Desktop.

You want to use it against **two backends at once** — a remote **DGX Spark**
(GB10, vLLM/CUDA) and your **MacBook Pro** running **oMLX** (MLX models, port 8000)
and **llama.cpp** (GGUF) — and you want live insight into **decode tok/s, TTFT,
prefill t/s, requests, VRAM, power, GPU util, temp**, plus a **left artifacts
sidebar** that renders the markdown/code/HTML your models produce.

**Decisions locked in (from Q&A):**
- **Stack: Tauri** — keep the Rust core (chat, streaming, SQLite, tools, config),
  replace the egui UI with a React/TS web frontend. ~15–25 MB binary, native perf.
- **Scope: connect-only first** — point at already-running servers; show dashboard +
  chat + artifacts. Process spawn/evict ("Stop"/MODELS dropdown) is a later phase.
- **Local GGUF: llama.cpp**; **local MLX: oMLX**. Both run side-by-side on the Mac.
- **Spark metrics: small agent (recommended below)** — see Metrics section.

## Target architecture

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Frontend  src/  — React + TypeScript + Vite    │
│   • Status dashboard (uPlot charts)            │
│   • Chat view (markdown, branching, tools)     │
│   • Artifacts sidebar (react-markdown + shiki) │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↕ Tauri commands (invoke) + events (emit)
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Rust core  src-tauri/src/                      │
│   core/api.rs        ← reuse (streaming, SSE)  │
│   core/message.rs    ← reuse as-is             │
│   core/storage.rs    ← reuse as-is (SQLite)    │
│   core/config.rs     ← reuse + extend (backends)│
│   core/commands.rs   ← reuse (slash cmds)      │
│   core/markdown.rs   ← reuse (segmenting)      │
│   core/tools.rs      ← reuse (tool exec)       │
│   metrics/*          ← NEW (scrape + gpu)      │
│   commands.rs        ← NEW Tauri command layer │
│   lib.rs / main.rs   ← NEW Tauri setup + state │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Reuse map (~70% of existing Rust survives):**
- `src/api.rs`, `message.rs`, `storage.rs`, `config.rs`, `commands.rs`,
  `markdown.rs`, `tools.rs` → move under `src-tauri/src/core/` largely unchanged.
- `src/app.rs` (3,289 lines of egui) and `src/main.rs` → **discarded**, replaced by
  a Tauri command layer + a Rust `AppState` (the non-UI fields of today's `ChatApp`:
  `Storage`, current conversation, loaded tools, saved backends, etc.).
- Streaming changes shape only: instead of `mpsc` drained per egui frame
  (`process_events`), `stream_chat`'s `StreamEvent`s are forwarded to the frontend
  via a Tauri `Channel`/`emit` keyed by a request id. The SSE parser in `api.rs` is
  unchanged.

## New: backends model (config.rs extension)

**`config.toml` stays the source of truth** and remains hand-editable; the existing
load/save/sanitize path in `config.rs` is kept (TOML, range-clamping, broken-file
backup). Endpoints stay **fully customizable** — arbitrary URLs/ports, per-endpoint
API keys, add/remove from the UI *and* by editing the file. Generalize today's
`Endpoint { url, api_key }` into a **Backend** (backward-compatible: a bare
`{url, api_key}` still parses, defaulting `runtime = OpenAi`, no metrics):

```
Backend {
  id, name,                       // "spark", "mac-omlx", "openrouter"
  base_url, api_key,              // OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint (any host/port)
  runtime: Vllm | Omlx | LlamaCpp | OpenAi,   // OpenAi = generic/cloud
  prometheus_url: Option<String>, // /metrics scrape (vLLM, llama.cpp --metrics)
  gpu_source: None | Macmon | SparkAgent{url} | Ssh{host},
}
```

Seed examples covering all three of your real backends:
- **Spark** — remote vLLM, `gpu_source = SparkAgent`, `prometheus_url` set.
- **mac-omlx** — `base_url = http://localhost:42069/v1` (your custom oMLX port, **not**
  the 8000 default), `runtime = Omlx`, `gpu_source = Macmon`, no prometheus (oMLX
  metrics come from client-measured per-request).
- **mac-llamacpp** — local GGUF, `runtime = LlamaCpp`, `gpu_source = Macmon`,
  `prometheus_url` set (`--metrics`).
- **openrouter** — `runtime = OpenAi`, `gpu_source = None`, no prometheus; cloud
  chat works exactly as today (OpenRouter URL auto-fix logic in `api.rs` is kept).

**Graceful degradation by backend:** the dashboard renders whatever a backend
provides. Cloud/OpenAI backends (OpenRouter) show **only** client-measured tiles
(TTFT, decode, prefill, tokens, cost) and hide the GPU/server-scrape sections rather
than showing empty boxes. So OpenRouter remains a normal, fully usable chat endpoint.

## New: metrics subsystem (`src-tauri/src/metrics/`)

Three independent collectors feed a shared `MetricsState`; a poller task emits a
snapshot to the frontend every ~1 s via Tauri events. Charts keep a rolling
~30-min ring buffer (mirrors the screenshot's "LAST 30 MINUTES").

1. **Client-measured per-request** (`metrics/request.rs`) — works for *every*
   backend incl. oMLX, no server support needed. Wrap our own `stream_chat`:
   - **TTFT** = first-token time − request-send time.
   - **decode tok/s** = completion_tokens / (done − first_token).
   - **prefill t/s** = prompt_tokens / TTFT.
   These populate the big tiles for the active conversation's last request.

2. **Server scrape** (`metrics/prometheus.rs`) — poll `prometheus_url`, parse the
   Prometheus text format (small hand-rolled parser; no heavy dep). Map:
   - vLLM: `vllm:generation_tokens_total` & `vllm:prompt_tokens_total` (rate →
     decode/prefill tok/s), `vllm:time_to_first_token_seconds` (histogram → TTFT),
     `vllm:num_requests_running`/`_waiting`, `vllm:gpu_cache_usage_perc` (KV).
   - llama.cpp (`--metrics`): `llamacpp:predicted_tokens_seconds`,
     `llamacpp:prompt_tokens_seconds`, `*_total` counters.
   Gives server-wide view (incl. requests not from this app) + the totals row.

3. **GPU / system** (`metrics/gpu.rs`):
   - **Mac (local):** `macmon` crate (`cargo add macmon`, no sudo) → GPU power (W),
     GPU util/residency, temp, unified memory. Drives VRAM/POWER/TEMP tiles locally.
   - **Spark (remote):** **recommended — ship a tiny `hchat-agent` binary** (second
     `[[bin]]` target in the workspace, reuses our Rust). It runs
     `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=power.draw,power.limit,temperature.gpu,utilization.gpu
     --format=csv,noheader,nounits` **plus reads `/proc/meminfo`** and serves JSON
     over HTTP. **Why both:** on GB10/DGX Spark `nvidia-smi` returns power ✓ and
     temp ✓ but **memory = "Not Supported"** (unified memory), so VRAM must come from
     `/proc/meminfo` (MemTotal/MemAvailable) — same approach vllm-studio uses for
     Jetson. One static ARM64 binary you `scp` to the Spark and run; low latency,
     correct VRAM. **Fallback** (zero install): `gpu_source: Ssh{host}` runs the same
     two commands over SSH instead. (This agent is just a metrics exporter — it does
     **not** manage inference servers, so it stays within "connect-only first".)

**Recommendation for your Spark question (vram + power + temp):** use the
`hchat-agent` sidecar. It's the only option that gets you *all three* on GB10,
because plain `nvidia-smi` can't report unified-memory VRAM and vLLM `/metrics`
has no GPU power/temp at all.

## New: frontend (`src/`, React + TS + Vite)

Layout mirrors the screenshot + Claude Desktop:
- **Left rail:** Workspace nav (Status · Usage · Models · Server), Projects/
  conversations list (reusing `storage.rs` conversation list, search, pin, rename,
  branching), Settings. Terminal-minimal dark theme, monospace numerics.
- **Status view:** ACTIVE bar (runtime/badge/port, Stop disabled for now, MODELS,
  LOGS, BENCH), big tiles **DECODE / TTFT / PREFILL** with peak sub-labels, **REQ /
  VRAM / POWER** tiles, totals row (total/prompt/completion tokens, duration), two
  time-series charts **THROUGHPUT (tok/s)** and **TTFT (ms) & REQUESTS**, a **GPUS**
  list (per-GPU util/mem/temp/power bars), and a **CONTROLLER LOGS** tail.
- **Chat view:** reuse streaming/markdown/branching/tools/presets/attachments. Code
  blocks, `<think>` reasoning collapse, copy buttons — port the behavior, restyle.
- **Artifacts sidebar (left, collapsible):** detect fenced code / HTML / SVG /
  mermaid in assistant messages and render them as selectable artifacts;
  `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `shiki` for rendering. Click a code block in
  chat → opens in the artifact panel, like Claude Desktop.

Charts: **uPlot** (tiny, fast, fits the minimal aesthetic). Markdown: react-markdown
+ shiki. No component framework lock-in; hand-styled CSS to match the screenshot.

## Build phases (execution order)

- **A — Tauri scaffold + core port.** `npm create tauri-app` (React/TS/Vite); move
  the 7 reusable Rust modules under `core/`; build the Tauri command layer
  (`list_conversations`, `load_conversation`, `send_message` w/ streaming channel,
  `fetch_models`, settings/presets/tools, slash commands). Result: chat works
  end-to-end in the web UI with SQLite history, branching, streaming, tools — feature
  parity with today, new shell.
- **B — Metrics dashboard.** Backends config; the three collectors; poller→events;
  Status view with tiles, charts, GPU list, logs tail. Includes the `hchat-agent`
  bin + a one-liner deploy note for the Spark.
- **C — Artifacts sidebar.** Artifact detection + rendering panel.
- **D — (later, out of scope now)** process spawn/evict/recipes (the real "manage"
  scope) — design left open; backends model already leaves room for it.

## Things to verify during implementation

- Confirm whether **oMLX** exposes a Prometheus `/metrics` (docs show streaming usage
  stats but not a scrape endpoint). If not, oMLX relies on client-measured metrics
  only (collector #1) — already the plan's assumption.
- Pin the **macmon** library API (it ships a Rust lib + Prometheus server; confirm the
  sampler call returns GPU power/temp/mem in the fields we need).
- Confirm **llama.cpp** build has `--metrics`; confirm exact metric names on the
  installed version (names have churned across releases).
- DGX Spark: confirm `utilization.gpu` is populated (some GB10 NVML fields read
  "Not Supported"); if so, derive util from the agent or omit gracefully.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. `cargo build` (workspace: app + `hchat-agent`) and `npm run tauri dev` launch the app.
2. **Chat parity:** create a conversation against the Mac oMLX endpoint, stream a
   reply, branch/regenerate, run a tool, reload — history persists (same `hchat.db`).
3. **Local metrics:** with oMLX or llama.cpp running, Status shows live DECODE/TTFT/
   PREFILL from a sent message, and VRAM/POWER/TEMP from macmon; charts fill over time.
4. **Spark metrics:** run `hchat-agent` on the Spark, point the Spark backend at it +
   vLLM `/metrics`; Status shows GPU power/temp/util + unified-memory VRAM and
   server-side throughput/requests; CONTROLLER LOGS tails.
5. **Artifacts:** ask a model for an HTML/SVG/code artifact → it renders in the left
   sidebar; clicking a chat code block opens it there.
6. `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; run the review subagent + PR ritual per
   delivery standards before merging.

name = "hchat"

content.text: The Tauri context embeds app icons at compile time, so I need real icon files. Let me generate a source PNG, then install deps (including the Tauri CLI) and let it produce all icon formats.

input.command:
cd /Users/heath/code/hChat
mkdir -p src-tauri/icons
python3 - <<'PY'
import zlib, struct
W=H=512
# hChat brand: dark charcoal bg with a soft teal rounded square + ghost-ish dot
bg=(18,18,20); fg=(56,189,180)
def px(x,y):
    # rounded-square glyph centered
    cx,cy=W//2,H//2; half=150; r=46
    dx=abs(x-cx); dy=abs(y-cy)
    inside = dx<=half and dy<=half
    if inside:
        # rounded corners
        if dx>half-r and dy>half-r:
            if (dx-(half-r))**2+(dy-(half-r))**2 > r*r:
                return bg
        # inner "chat" cutout (small notch bottom-left to suggest a bubble tail)
        return fg
    return bg
raw=bytearray()
for y in range(H):
    raw.append(0)
    for x in range(W):
        raw.extend(px(x,y))
def chunk(typ,data):
    c=struct.pack(">I",len(data))+typ+data
    return c+struct.pack(">I",zlib.crc32(typ+data)&0xffffffff)
png=b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n"
png+=chunk(b"IHDR",struct.pack(">IIBBBBB",W,H,8,2,0,0,0))
png+=chunk(b"IDAT",zlib.compress(bytes(raw),9))
png+=chunk(b"IEND",b"")
open("src-tauri/icons/source.png","wb").write(png)
print("wrote source.png", len(png), "bytes")
PY

content.subject: Phase A: Tauri scaffold + core port to chat parity
content.description: Lay out Tauri 2 project (root React/TS/Vite frontend + src-tauri Rust). Move existing src/*.rs into src-tauri/src/core/. Build AppState + Tauri command layer (conversations, load/save, send_message with streaming channel, fetch_models, settings/presets/tools, slash commands). Result: chat works end-to-end in web UI with SQLite history, branching, streaming, tools.

content.subject: Phase A-UI: React frontend chat shell
content.description: Left rail (workspace nav + conversation list/search/pin/rename), chat view with streaming, markdown rendering (react-markdown+shiki), reasoning collapse, code copy, attachments, branching nav, presets, settings panel. Terminal-minimal dark theme.

content.subject: Phase B: Metrics subsystem + Status dashboard
content.description: Backend model in config.rs (runtime, prometheus_url, gpu_source). Three collectors: client-measured per-request (TTFT/decode/prefill), Prometheus scrape (vLLM/llama.cpp), GPU (macmon local + hchat-agent bin for Spark via nvidia-smi + /proc/meminfo). Poller emits snapshots via Tauri events. Status view: tiles, uPlot charts, GPU list, logs tail.

content.subject: Phase C: Artifacts sidebar
content.description: Detect fenced code/HTML/SVG/mermaid in assistant messages, render as selectable artifacts in a collapsible left panel like Claude Desktop. Click a chat code block to open it in the artifact panel.

content.text:
## Status — Phase A foundation is built and running ✅

I restructured hChat into a Tauri app and got a working end-to-end chat client booting. The dev window is open right now (background process `bdeau4mor`).

**What's done:**
- **Tauri layout**: React/TS/Vite frontend (root `src/`) + Rust backend (`src-tauri/`). Your 7 reusable core modules (`api`, `message`, `storage`, `config`, `slash`, `markdown`, `tools`) moved under `src-tauri/src/core/` and compile unchanged via `#[path]`. The egui `app.rs` is gone (in git history).
- **Command layer** (`src-tauri/src/commands.rs`) replacing the egui loop: streaming `send_message` (forwards tokens over a Tauri `Channel`), conversations CRUD, history with your branching-aware storage, models fetch, config save, auto-title, cancel. API keys are resolved server-side from `config.toml` — never round-trip through JS.
- **React UI**: left rail (Status/Chat nav + conversation list with pin/rename/delete/search), chat view with live streaming, markdown rendering (react-markdown + shiki highlighting), collapsible `<think>` reasoning, per-code-block copy, model + endpoint pickers, Settings modal (endpoints + keys + generation defaults), and a Status dashboard scaffold matching the screenshot's tile/chart/GPU/logs layout.
- **config.toml preserved** — your existing endpoints (oMLX `:42069`, deepthought `:8080`, ollama) load as-is; OpenRouter path kept intact.

**Verified**: Rust + TS + Vite all compile clean (one harmless dead-code warning for the not-yet-wired `tools` field); the app launches and stays up.

**To try it now**: in the open window, switch the endpoint dropdown to `http://localhost:42069/v1` (oMLX is live; your default `deepthought:8080` isn't reachable from here) and send a message. To stop the dev server later: `kill 90982` or Ctrl-C in the terminal — or just `npm run tauri dev` to relaunch.

**Not yet (tracked as tasks #5, #6, and Phase B/C):**
- Tool calling in chat (approval flow + execution loop) — backend currently passes `tools: None`
- Branching (regenerate/edit/sibling nav), attachments, presets — storage already supports these
- **Phase B**: the real metrics — macmon GPU stats, vLLM/llama.cpp `/metrics` scrape, and the `hchat-agent` for the Spark. Right now the Status tiles show client-measured decode/TTFT/prefill from your own requests; VRAM/power show "—".

This is a natural checkpoint. Want me to keep going straight through tools → branching → Phase B metrics, or would you rather poke at the running app first and steer the design/direction before I build further? (Note: I haven't committed anything — it's all staged on the `rewrite-tauri` branch for you to review.)
